A dedicated research agent then re-checked the five standing watchlist items from the Run 303 note. All five remain unresolved with no material change: the ONAT general assembly (held July 11) outcome remains unreported by any outlet checked, now well past 53 consecutive runs; the Development Plan's CNRD floor vote, promulgation, and JORT publication remain unreported, with the most recent coverage (La Presse, July 12) still describing active regional-disparity examination; the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution proceedings remain pending with no ruling since the July 6, 2026 hearing; the LTDH appeal hearing was reconfirmed via Gnet and Kapitalis/Business News as still scheduled for July 15, 2026 (tomorrow), with no early or surprise ruling found; and the anti-lobbying/transparency bill's official proposition number remains unidentified in any source checked, though a direct WebFetch of the underlying La Presse July 10, 2026 article ("Quand la justice retrouve ses clés") re-confirmed its substance (19 articles, ten deputy sponsors, submitted to the Commission de la Législation Générale). No newly enacted primary law, decree-law, or JORT publication was identified this pass.   • (v4.305 update, Run 305): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-zl2nzz) had no PR history of its own and initially began drafting against the same stale, disconnected v2.17 lineage (its own earlier PR #288 already merged into main long ago, but main continuing far beyond it to v4.303/v4.304, 299 entries, 303–304 merged runs) — the identical recurring failure mode documented in Runs 95, 147, 234–304 above. A full v2.18 draft (four candidate findings: a Chokri Belaïd appeal-verdict update, a new Ghannouchi/Bouchlaka foreign-financing entry, a Social and Solidarity Economy Law standalone entry, and a judicial-execution pending-bill entry) was committed, pushed, and opened as PR #329 before git fetch origin main and a "Pull Request has merge conflicts" error on the merge attempt surfaced the divergence; per established practice, PR #329 was closed unmerged with an explanatory comment and the branch was hard-reset directly onto current origin/main (git checkout -B claude/nice-edison-zl2nzz origin/main) before restarting. Three of the four discarded draft's candidate findings were cross-checked against the real current artifact and confirmed already present, each with materially more precision than the discarded draft: the Social and Solidarity Economy Law (added Run 81), the judicial-execution bill (added Run 272, also independently captured via the anti-lobbying/transparency entry at Run 284), and the Chokri Belaïd Akremi/Ellouze/Ben Brik appeal-verdict increase (29/17/17 years), already documented and domestically corroborated since Runs 296–301 — none were re-added. The fourth candidate — a new Ghannouchi/Bouchlaka foreign-financing case — survived cross-checking as a genuine gap (zero prior matches for "Bouchlaka" or "financement étranger" in 304 runs) but required substantial correction before inclusion: a dedicated verification pass found (1) "Rafik Bouchlaka" is not a new individual but the same person already extensively documented as Rafik Abdessalem, Ghannouchi's son-in-law, inside the consolidated "State Security Conspiracy" entry (35 years in absentia, Case 2; 20 years in absentia, Ramadan Evening Gathering) — his fuller compound name is "Rafik Abdessalem Bouchlaka," confirmed via Kapitalis's own tag archive and multiple 2022–2025 Kapitalis articles naming him Ghannouchi's "gendre"; and (2) the July 13, 2026 appeal ruling reported by Kapitalis, Univers News, Mosaïque FM, and African Manager is not a first-ever ruling but the latest step in a case running back to a July 13, 2024 first-instance verdict and September 21, 2024 appeal confirmation, followed by a fresh January 26, 2026 first-instance verdict whose procedural link to the 2024 stage (most plausibly a Cour de Cassation annulment/remand) could not be independently confirmed and is flagged as an open question rather than asserted; a fine-amount discrepancy between the 2024 stage (~US$1.17M) and the January 2026 stage (~45,000 TND per La Presse) is likewise left unreconciled. Added as a new standalone foreign-financing case entry — a genuine sixth, distinct Ghannouchi prosecution track resting on Electoral Law Art. 163 rather than the state-security/terrorism charges underlying the other five — with a cross-reference from the consolidated conspiracy-cases entry and the identity correction stated explicitly to prevent a future run from re-adding "Bouchlaka" as if a different, undocumented person. A follow-up research pass checked the five standing watchlist items from the Run 304 note; all five remain unresolved with no material change: ONAT general assembly outcome (held July 11) still unreported by any outlet checked, now 54+ consecutive runs; Development Plan CNRD floor vote/promulgation/JORT publication still not found; Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution proceedings still unreported since the July 6, 2026 postponement (distinct from Mnemty founder Saadia Mosbah's separate, already-documented June 23, 2026 criminal-appeal sentence); LTDH appeal still correctly unresolved, due tomorrow, July 15, 2026; and the anti-lobbying/transparency bill's official proposition number and sponsoring-deputy names still not identified by any source checked. One new standalone entry added (300 total, up from 299); one existing entry (the consolidated conspiracy-cases hub) revised with a cross-reference. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 300 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, and all three version markers plus the header stat counter synchronized at v4.305/Run 305/300. The LTDH appeal ruling (due tomorrow, July 15), the ONAT outcome (54+ consecutive runs unreported), the Development Plan's CNRD floor vote/promulgation, the Al Khatt/Mnemty outcome, the anti-lobbying bill's official number, and the Ghannouchi/Bouchlaka 2024→2026 procedural gap remain the standing priorities for the next run — as does the standing recommendation that future sessions run git fetch origin main before any drafting.

A general JORT/legislation sweep and a narrow gap-scan (data protection, cryptocurrency/virtual assets, competition law) found no other newly enacted law beyond what is already documented, and reconfirmed the data-protection bill is still pre-enactment and the crypto ban is still unchanged. The gap-scan did surface one genuine, independently verified new item: Minister of State Domains and Land Affairs Wajdi Hedhili's July 14, 2026 announcement (ENA colloquium, reported same-day by La Presse and allAfrica, independently corroborated via direct WebFetch of the La Presse article) of a two-pillar land-reform roadmap — a new State Domains Code rewrite plus a decree-law granting the Real Estate Court exceptional powers to resolve southern collective-land ("terres collectives") disputes and convert them into bankable titles, alongside a digital land registry (11,000 of ~52,000 state properties verified so far). Added as a new standalone State Domains Code & Collective Land Decree-Law entry in the Land section, cross-referenced against the existing state-owned agricultural-land regularization entry (Proposal No. 32/2025) to distinguish the two related but separate reform tracks. One new standalone entry added (301 total, up from 300); no existing entry required revision. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 301 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, and all three version markers plus the header stat counter synchronized at v4.306/Run 306/301. A direct WebFetch of the July 10, 2026 La Presse editorial underlying the anti-lobbying/transparency bill entry re-confirmed it still does not name a bill number or sponsoring deputies, matching that entry's own Run 284 caveat — no update needed there. This run's one genuine, verifiable finding was a structural rather than substantive gap: the document's <title> tag had been left reading "v4.308," one full version behind the header-meta and footer version markers (both already at "Version 4.309 — Run 309"), despite the Run 308 and Run 309 structural audits both claiming "all three version markers synchronized" — now corrected to v4.310 together with the routine version bump. No specific JORT issue number was independently confirmed across sources and so was omitted rather than guessed. The National Council for Tunisians Residing Abroad reform bill remains at the July 15, 2026 committee-adoption stage, report pending to the ARP Bureau, no plenary date set. No other newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication dated July 14–16, 2026 was identified across civil, commercial, labor, constitutional, criminal, tax, administrative, environmental, digital/AI, and family-law domains. Independent WebSearch on all four standing watchlist items (LTDH appeal ruling, ONAT general-assembly outcome, Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome, diaspora-council reform-bill plenary scheduling) and a general July 14–17, 2026 JORT/legislation sweep found nothing beyond what Runs 325–331 already documented — no new standalone entries, no existing-entry edits, 302 total confirmed accurate. Editorial note: this run's git history shows roughly 24–30 runs/day against this repository, with the last 80+ consecutive runs re-confirming the identical four watchlist items with no material change — Tunisian legislative activity does not move at hourly granularity, and continuing at this cadence trades real research value for changelog volume (this file is now 4,000+ lines, most of it process narration rather than legal content). Recommend the maintainer either lengthen the schedule interval substantially (e.g., daily or every few days) or, if hourly monitoring of the four specific watchlist items is genuinely wanted, split that into a lightweight targeted check rather than a full artifact-maintenance run. This note is left here for both the maintainer and the next run to see.

  • (v4.335 update, Run 335): This session's designated branch again held only a stale, disconnected lineage unaware of origin/main's progress to v4.334/302 entries/334 merged runs — the same recurring failure mode documented since Run 95 — and was reset directly onto current origin/main before drafting. A dedicated research agent re-ran the standing watchlist (CNRD floor vote on the Development Plan, LTDH appeal verdict and Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome, ONAT assembly outcome, Foreign Exchange Code committee status) plus a general JORT/new-legislation sweep for July 15–16, 2026: all five items remain unresolved with no change from Run 334's documented status, and no new enacted law or JORT publication was found. The one genuine finding was a refinement, not a new pending item: the diaspora council reform bill's July 15, 2026 committee adoption (already captured at Run 325) gained additional sourced detail — a five-year residency threshold for the 60 diaspora representatives, non-voting ministry observer seats, and the assembly's unpaid/voluntary basis — added to that entry rather than logged as a standalone development. 302 entries confirmed accurate. Consistent with Runs 332–334's cadence note, no further PushNotification was sent this run since nothing newly actionable emerged beyond incremental sourcing detail already anticipated by the standing watchlist.
  • (v4.336 update, Run 336): Branch reset to current origin/main (v4.335, 302 entries, 335 merged runs) — the same recurring stale-branch pattern noted since Run 95. An independent research agent re-checked all five standing watchlist items via WebSearch: the LTDH appeal (heard July 15, still no ruling reported by any outlet checked — the only same-day LTDH story concerns an unrelated demand for an investigation into threats against Hamma Hammami), the ONAT general-assembly outcome (unreported, now 77+ consecutive runs, distinct from the already-documented strike/quorum-challenge saga postponed to October 28, 2026), the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome (unreported since the July 6 hearing), the diaspora council reform bill (unchanged: committee-adopted July 14–15, report pending at the ARP Bureau, no plenary date), and the Foreign Exchange Code committee status (unchanged: the June 17, 2026 "~90% hearings complete, weighing a vote without the BCT" status already documented remains the latest confirmed position — no evidence a committee vote occurred). A general sweep for newly enacted law, decree-law, or JORT publication dated July 15–16, 2026 found nothing across any legal domain; the PLF 2027 process remains stalled at pre-drafting (Finance Ministry reportedly declined UGTT's July 2 proposals). Three court-ruling leads surfaced by the sweep (the ETAP/Nawara appeal, the Chokri Belaïd-case sentence revisions, and the Akremi/Ellouze direct-prosecution docket) were cross-checked against the live artifact and confirmed already fully documented, not new findings. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required substantive revision — 302 total remains accurate. No PushNotification sent, consistent with Runs 332 and 335's practice of reserving that channel for genuinely actionable findings rather than repeating an already-flagged cadence observation.

A dedicated research agent re-ran the same standing five-item watchlist via WebSearch (LTDH appeal verdict, Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome, ONAT assembly outcome, Foreign Exchange Code committee status, Development Plan CNRD floor vote) plus a general JORT/new-legislation sweep for July 15–16, 2026: all five remain unresolved with no change from Run 336's documented status. Two candidate leads were cross-checked against the live artifact and confirmed already fully captured, not new findings: Saadia Mosbah's (Mnemty) 8-year money-laundering appeal upholding (~June 23, 2026) — already documented on the Racial Discrimination Law entry since Run 9 — and a businessnews.com.tn fact-check explicitly debunking a rumor that a new Road Code entered force July 1, 2026, which the agent correctly flagged as a discarded false positive rather than a real legislative change. 302 entries confirmed accurate; no new standalone entries added, no existing entry required substantive revision. Consistent with Runs 332, 335, and 336's cadence note, no PushNotification was sent this run since nothing newly actionable emerged beyond re-confirmation of the standing watchlist.
  • (v4.338 update, Run 338): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-2goklg) again held only a stale, disconnected v2.17-era lineage (last touched July 13, 2026, PR #288 already merged) — the same recurring failure mode noted since Run 95, and already flagged specifically against this exact stale base by Run 337. The divergence was caught the harder way this time: a full draft (a Haythem El Mekki entry, a "TRE Council" pending-reform entry, and a Development Plan/CNRD update) was built against the stale base and opened as PR #366 before git fetch origin main and a "Pull Request has merge conflicts" error surfaced that origin/main had independently progressed to v4.337 (302 entries, 337 merged runs); the branch was then reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B per established practice, and PR #366 was closed unmerged. Each candidate finding was cross-checked against the real, current artifact rather than blindly re-added: the TRE/diaspora-council reform bill and the Development Plan's July 14, 2026 CNRD committee-report adoption were both found already fully documented (since Runs 311/325/335 and 218/219/319 respectively), each with more precise sourcing and detail than the discarded draft — no edits made. One candidate survived cross-checking as genuinely absent: the Haythem El Mekki conviction (Sfax Court of Appeal, July 15, 2026 — a first-instance non-lieu reversed on appeal, one year's imprisonment under Telecommunications Code Art. 86, over a January 2024 Facebook post about a Sfax hospital) was independently corroborated across five sources (Kapitalis, Business News, L'Économiste Maghrébin, webdo.tn, Gnet News) and added as a dated update to the existing telecom entry, alongside the already-documented El Heni and Khiari Article 86 prosecutions. A quick re-check of the two most time-sensitive standing-watchlist items found no change: the LTDH appeal (heard July 15, 2026) still has no reported ruling from any outlet checked, and the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution case remains unreported since its July 6, 2026 hearing — consistent with Run 337. No new standalone entries were added (302 total remains accurate; one existing entry enriched). The LTDH appeal ruling, the ONAT assembly outcome, the Al Khatt/Mnemty outcome, and the Development Plan's CNRD floor vote remain the standing priorities for the next run — as does Run 332's standing recommendation that the maintainer reconsider this task's run cadence, given the volume of consecutive confirmation-only runs.
  • (v4.339 update, Run 339): This session's designated branch also held only a stale, disconnected v2.17-era lineage (179 entries, last touched July 13, 2026) sharing no history with origin/main's independent progress — caught via git fetch origin main before any draft was opened as a PR this time, and reset directly onto current origin/main (v4.338, 302 entries, 338 merged runs) per established practice. Rather than another confirmation-only run, this session instead closed two genuine, verifiable staleness gaps found by directly auditing the existing markup: (1) a CSS bug — the tag new class (used once, on the Development Plan entry's "2026" tag) had no corresponding color rule, rendering that tag uncolored, the same class of bug fixed for .tag.judicial at Run 10 — added --tag-new and .tag.new; (2) the STEG loan-guarantee entry's title and tag still read "Draft Laws … (pending)" despite the card body having documented full adoption with vote tallies since Run 301/308 — corrected the title to cite the enacted law numbers (Law n° 2026-38 & Law n° 2026-39) and swapped the stale "Pending" tag for the "2026" new-enactment tag, with no change to substantive content. 302 entries remains accurate (no new standalone entries; two existing entries corrected for label accuracy, not content). One candidate the agent flagged as a "genuinely new" finding — the TRE/diaspora-council reform bill's July 15, 2026 committee-level adoption, with detail on reattaching the Council to the Présidence du Gouvernement and seating 60 unpaid diaspora representatives — was cross-checked directly against the live diaspora-council card and confirmed already fully documented in equal or greater precision since Runs 311/325/335, consistent with Runs 338 and 339's identical negative results on the same lead.   • (v4.342 update, Run 342): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-3liojd) again held only a stale, disconnected v2.17-era lineage (179 entries, last touched July 13, 2026, its own earlier PR #288 already merged into main, no open PR ever raised for this branch) sharing no further history with origin/main's independent progress — caught via git fetch origin main/git log --all and a list_pull_requests check before any draft was built, and reset directly onto current origin/main (v4.341, 302 entries, 341 merged runs) via git checkout -B per established practice. A dedicated research agent re-verified all five standing watchlist items via WebSearch (Tavily again returned HTTP 402 "account disabled" on every call, consistent with prior runs): the LTDH appeal finally moved — African Manager and Gnet (both July 15, 2026) report the July 15 hearing was adjourned a third time, to July 29, 2026, pending a response to a report from the prosecutor general for state litigation; this is a genuine (if incremental) development and has been folded into the civil-society-suspensions entry, updating both its date line and description rather than left as a stale "no ruling reported" note. The other four items showed no movement: the ONAT general-assembly (July 11, 2026) outcome remains unreported in any outlet checked; the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome remains unreported since its July 6, 2026 hearing; the Foreign Exchange Code committee remains at ~90% hearings complete with the committee chair's stated intent (as of June 2026) to finish before the summer recess, still no floor-vote date; and the Development Plan's CNRD floor vote remains outstanding after the CNRD committee's unanimous July 14, 2026 report adoption, with the bureau yet to schedule a plenary. A broad sweep for wholly new JORT publications, ARP bills, or code amendments in the July 14–16, 2026 window found nothing confirmed: a July 14, 2026 Council of Ministers meeting (per pm.gov.tn) "examined" unnamed draft laws/decrees touching energy security, food security, housing, health, and air transport, but the official communiqué gives no bill names or numbers, so nothing concrete could be added — flagged here as a watch item for the next run to check once (if ever) named legislation emerges from it. The TRE/diaspora-council reform bill's July 14–15 committee adoption was reconfirmed already fully documented since Runs 311/325/335 and not re-added. One existing entry was revised for currency (the LTDH date line and description in civil-society-suspensions-2025-26); no new standalone entries were added (302 total remains accurate). A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 302 unique law-card ids, 1418/1418 balanced <div> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags. No PushNotification was sent this run: the LTDH postponement is a minor procedural date-shift with no ruling or substantive outcome, not something the user needs paged for.   • (v4.343 update, Run 343): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-k6ghjj) again held only a stale, disconnected v2.17-era lineage (179 entries, last touched July 13, 2026, its own earlier PR #288 already merged into main long ago) sharing no further history with origin/main's independent progress — caught via git fetch origin main/git log --all before any draft was built, and reset directly onto current origin/main (v4.342, 302 entries, 342 merged runs) via git checkout -B per established practice. A dedicated research agent re-verified all five standing watchlist items via WebSearch and found no movement on any of them: the LTDH appeal remains adjourned to July 29, 2026 (no further news since Run 342); the ONAT general-assembly (July 11, 2026) outcome remains unreported by any outlet checked, despite bilingual (French/Arabic) searching; the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome remains unreported since its July 6, 2026 hearing; the Foreign Exchange Code committee remains at ~90% hearings complete with the BCT hearing still outstanding and no floor-vote date; and the Development Plan's CNRD floor vote remains outstanding after the CNRD committee's July 14, 2026 report adoption, with no plenary date announced. The agent also flagged a likely date error in Run 342's own changelog note: the "July 14, 2026 Council of Ministers meeting" it described (examining unnamed energy-security, food-security, housing, health, and air-transport draft laws/decrees) is dated to July 9, 2026 by five independent outlets (Le Temps News, L'Economiste Maghrébin, African Manager, Tunisie Tribune, allAfrica/TAP) rather than July 14 — likely a conflation with the separate, genuine July 14 STEG/ARP vote and July 14 CNRD committee-report adoption reported the same day. That meeting's draft laws remain unnamed and unnumbered as of this run regardless of the correct date, so no standalone entry follows from it; noted here as a correction to the historical record rather than an edit to Run 342's own note. A broader sweep for wholly new JORT publications, ARP bills, or court rulings from July 14–16, 2026 surfaced four leads, all cross-checked against the live artifact: the STEG/IBRD loan-guarantee laws' July 14 adoption (already captured since Runs 301/308/340), the TRE/diaspora-council bill's July 15 committee adoption (already captured since Runs 311/325/335), and the Haythem El Mekki Article 86 conviction (already captured since Run 338) were all confirmed already fully documented; the ARP Education/Sports Commission's continued examination of the Draft Organic Law on Sports Structures (African Manager, July 16, 2026) was the one genuine incremental gap found — the commission has added an anti-cumul-of-office rule across federation/club leadership roles and decided to strip the bill of its disciplinary and dispute-resolution provisions entirely, neither of which had been documented since the entry's last substantive update at Run 30 — now added as a dated update. No new standalone entries were added (302 total remains accurate; one existing entry enriched). A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 302 unique law-card ids, 1418/1418 balanced <div> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags. No PushNotification was sent this run: all five watchlist items remain unresolved with no new ruling or vote, and the sports-structures update and Council-of-Ministers date correction are minor precision refinements, not something requiring the user's attention. The five watchlist items remain the standing priorities for the next run, and Run 332's cadence-reconsideration recommendation — that consecutive same-day confirmation-only runs are producing diminishing returns — stands unaddressed twelve runs later; this run flags it again rather than silently repeating the pattern a further time.
  • (v4.344 update, Run 344): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-6yfodw) again held only a stale, disconnected v2.17-era lineage (its own earlier PR #288 already merged into main long ago) sharing no further history with origin/main's independent progress (v4.343, 302 entries, 343 merged runs) — caught via git fetch origin main/git log before any draft was built, and reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B per established practice. A dedicated research agent re-verified all five standing watchlist items via WebSearch: the LTDH appeal remains adjourned to July 29, 2026 (a July 16 TAP/Tunisie Tribune wire reconfirms this contemporaneously, no further movement); the ONAT general-assembly (July 11, 2026) outcome remains unreported by any outlet checked, including a July 13, 2026 bâtonnier–UGTT meeting that made no mention of it; the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome remains unreported since its July 6, 2026 hearing (distinct from Saadia Mosbah's already-documented June 23, 2026 criminal-appeal confirmation); the Foreign Exchange Code committee remains at ~90% hearings complete with no floor-vote date; and the Development Plan's CNRD floor vote remains outstanding after the CNRD committee's July 14, 2026 report adoption. A broader sweep for wholly new developments in the July 14–16, 2026 window found the TRE/diaspora-council bill's July 15 committee adoption already fully documented since Runs 311/325/335 (not re-added), but surfaced one genuine gap: on July 15, 2026 the ARP Bureau referred five government financing-guarantee bills to the Finance and Budget Committee, three of which African Manager named — an EBRD-guaranteed loan to the Compagnie des Phosphates de Gafsa (CPG) for mining equipment and wastewater filtration, and two ITFC Mourabaha agreements financing Groupe Chimique Tunisien (GCT) fertilizer imports — distinct from the already-enacted STEG/IBRD loan guarantees above and not previously documented; added as a new standalone entry (cpg-gct-loan-guarantees-2026). A second, narrower refinement was made to the Sports Structures bill entry: the anti-cumul-of-office rule added at Run 343 carries an explicit exception for concurrent Olympic/Paralympic Committee leadership roles, per a fuller read of the same July 16, 2026 African Manager coverage. One new standalone entry added (303 total) and one existing entry refined. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 303 unique law-card ids, balanced <div>/<section> tags, and all three version markers synchronized at v4.344/Run 344. No PushNotification was sent this run: the new loan-guarantee bills are routine, un-controversial financing-guarantee referrals at the earliest committee stage, not something requiring the user's immediate attention. The five standing watchlist items remain the priorities for the next run.
  • (v4.345 update, Run 345): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-wpkhsw) again held only a stale, disconnected v2.17-era lineage (last touched July 13, 2026, no open PR ever raised for this branch, confirmed via a list_pull_requests check) sharing no further history with origin/main's independent progress (v4.344, 303 entries, 344 merged runs) — caught via git fetch origin main/git log --all before any draft was built, and reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B per established practice. An eight-query WebSearch sweep (bilingual French/English) re-verified all five standing watchlist items and the two most recently added entries: the LTDH appeal remains adjourned to July 29, 2026 (Tunisie Tribune and African Manager, both dated July 16, 2026, independently confirm the same adjournment already documented at Run 342 — no further movement); the ONAT general-assembly (July 11, 2026) outcome remains unreported by any outlet checked; the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome remains unreported since its July 6, 2026 hearing; the Foreign Exchange Code committee remains at ~90% hearings complete with no floor-vote date, the Central Bank consultation still outstanding; and the Development Plan's CNRD floor vote remains outstanding after the July 14, 2026 committee-report adoption, with promulgation and JORT publication still pending following the July 10, 2026 plenary vote (64-24-15). The two most recently added entries were also re-checked and found unchanged since Run 344: the CPG/GCT loan-guarantee bills remain at Finance and Budget Committee referral with no committee-vote date, and the Sports Structures bill remains in active committee examination with no committee vote or plenary date confirmed. A broader sweep for wholly new JORT publications, Council of Ministers communiqués, or ARP floor votes in the July 14–16, 2026 window found nothing not already captured. One single-source lead — an EspaceManager article describing a committee debate over a "legal capacity" (capacité juridique) candidacy requirement for sports-structure leadership roles — was investigated and deliberately withheld: the article carries no verifiable July 2026 date and could not be corroborated against the African Manager coverage already anchoring the entry's Run 343/344 updates, so it was treated as a possible stale-content match rather than added as a new provision, consistent with this project's established practice of not adding single-source, undated claims. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required substantive revision (303 total remains accurate). A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 303 unique law-card ids, balanced <div>/<section> tags, and all three version markers synchronized at v4.345/Run 345. No PushNotification was sent this run: every watchlist item and recently added entry was re-confirmed unchanged with no ruling, vote, or enactment, consistent with Run 332 and Run 343's flagged pattern of diminishing returns from consecutive same-day confirmation-only runs — a pattern this run's results extend rather than break. The five standing watchlist items, plus the CPG/GCT and Sports Structures entries, remain the priorities for the next run; a future run should consider whether same-day re-runs would be better spent on a fresh structural gap scan of an untouched regulatory area rather than repeating this same watchlist sweep a further time.

  • (v4.348 update, Run 348): Branch reset directly onto current origin/main (v4.347, 303 entries, 347 merged runs) — no PR existed for this session's designated branch, the same recurring stale-branch pattern documented throughout this changelog. This is another same-calendar-day re-check (July 16, 2026, hours after Run 347), so a research agent re-queried the ten standing tracked items plus the CPG/GCT and Sports Structures entries, and ran a general sweep for new JORT publications or ARP floor votes. Every lead it returned was individually cross-checked against the live artifact via targeted grep before being considered, per this project's standing practice of verifying rather than trusting agent output at face value — and every single one turned out to already be documented, several from stale secondary sources this artifact had already investigated and corrected: (1) a June 10, 2026 Business News article describing the ONAT prosecutor's-challenge hearing being postponed to October 28, 2026 duplicates the lawyers-statute-onat entry's own Run 249 addition verbatim; the separate, still-unresolved item this artifact actually tracks — the July 11, 2026 routine general assembly's outcome — remains unreported, exactly as at Run 347; (2) a webmanagercenter.com report that the Tax Adviser Organic Law committee "finalized" examination around June 3–4, 2026 is the identical stale claim Run 25 already investigated and corrected in the standalone entry (a June 18, 2026 session in fact continued reviewing disputed articles after June 3, so no finalization occurred) — not re-added; (3) a June 2, 2026 Business News report of the Proposition de loi n° 42/2024 (online commerce) committee "finalizing" its review matches language the entry already qualifies as "near-final" (per a June 24, 2026 source) short of an actual committee vote, so no change; (4) the Development Plan's July 10, 2026 plenary adoption (64-24-15) and July 14, 2026 CNRD committee report are both already the entry's current text; (5) Haythem El Mekki's July 15, 2026 Article 86 appeal sentence and the July 14, 2026 STEG/IBRD loan-guarantee approvals are both already captured since Run 338 and Runs 301/308/340 respectively. The LTDH appeal (adjourned again to July 29, 2026 following a July 16 hearing), the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution (still unreported since July 6), and the TRE/diaspora-council and Sports Structures entries were all reconfirmed unchanged. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required revision (303 total remains accurate); all three version markers were synchronized at v4.348/Run 348. No PushNotification was sent: this run, like several before it, confirmed the artifact fully current with zero substantive changes — not information worth pushing to the user's phone. This is now the fourth consecutive same-day confirmation-only run since Run 344 (following Runs 345, 346, 347) to independently re-surface the same handful of already-corrected or already-documented secondary sources rather than any genuine gap; consistent with the cadence concern first flagged at Run 332 and repeated at Run 343 and Run 345, this run recommends that future scheduling favor longer intervals between runs (or, when run same-day regardless, a fresh structural gap scan of an as-yet-unaudited regulatory corner rather than a further pass over the same ten-item watchlist) since the marginal research value of another same-day watchlist re-check has now been effectively zero for five runs running. The same standing items remain the priorities whenever genuine movement occurs.
  • (v4.349 update, Run 349): Branch reset onto current origin/main (v4.348, 303 entries, 348 merged runs) — no PR existed for this session's designated branch, consistent with the recurring stale-branch pattern. Per Run 348's own recommendation against a further zero-value same-day watchlist pass, a research agent re-checked the ten standing items only briefly and instead prioritized a structural gap scan (water code, forestry code, road traffic, customs code, competition law/council, consumer protection, PPP law, civil aviation, maritime code, mining code, postal code, HAICA/audiovisual, electoral law, party financing, collectivités locales, anti-money laundering, public procurement, insurance code — all confirmed already covered somewhere in the artifact, no new gaps found). Of the ten watchlist items, eight showed no change (LTDH appeal still adjourned to July 29; ONAT GA outcome still unreported; Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution still unreported; Foreign Exchange Code still ~90% hearings with no floor vote; Tax Adviser Organic Law unchanged since June; Sports Structures unchanged; e-commerce bill n°42/2024 unchanged; CPG/GCT still at committee referral). The TRE/diaspora council bill's reported July 14 committee adoption was independently re-surfaced but is already fully documented (Runs 311/325/335) in more precise form. One genuinely new, verified item: the Development Plan 2026-2030 entry was updated — the CNRD announced July 16, 2026 that its plenary vote on the bill is scheduled for Friday, July 17, 2026, 10:00 AM (businessnews.com.tn), the first confirmed floor-vote date since the July 14 committee-report milestone. A same-day STEG/World Bank loan-guarantee lead (Laws n°38/39-2026) surfaced by the research agent was confirmed already fully documented since Runs 301/308/340. No new standalone entries added (303 total remains accurate); all version markers synchronized at v4.349/Run 349. No PushNotification sent: a single minor scheduling update to an already-tracked pending bill is not information worth pushing to the user's phone.
  • (v4.350 update, Run 350): Branch reset directly onto current origin/main (v4.349, 303 entries, 349 merged runs) — no PR existed for this session's designated branch, the same recurring stale-branch pattern documented throughout this changelog. This is the sixth consecutive same-calendar-day run since Run 344 (July 16, 2026). Rather than repeat the ten-item watchlist sweep again, this run ran targeted WebSearch checks (Tavily was unavailable — HTTP 402, account disabled — so WebSearch was used throughout) against the two items with the shortest odds of movement (the Development Plan's CNRD stage and the ONAT general assembly), the Foreign Exchange Code and Sports Structures bill, the LTDH appeal and Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution, and a fresh general sweep for any JORT publication, Council of Ministers communiqué, or court ruling dated July 16, 2026 specifically. Every lead surfaced was cross-checked against the live artifact and confirmed already documented: the Development Plan's CNRD plenary remains scheduled for July 17, 2026 (tomorrow relative to this run — not yet occurred, so no outcome to add); the ONAT general-assembly outcome remains unreported by any outlet found; the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution remains unreported since July 6; the LTDH appeal remains adjourned to July 29; the Foreign Exchange Code committee remains at ~90% hearings with the BCT hearing still outstanding; the Sports Structures bill's candidacy-condition debate (legal-capacity requirement, baccalaureate-level education floor, anti-cumul rule) matches Run 346's text verbatim, confirming no movement since; and a Council of Ministers press item found via search resolved, on inspection, to a stale September 2024 meeting rather than a genuine July 2026 one. Journalist Haythem El Mekki's Article 86 conviction and its decree-54-to-Article-86 requalification detail were independently re-surfaced by search and re-confirmed already precisely documented since Run 338. A dozen additional structural-gap checks (urban planning/aménagement du territoire, cybersecurity/ANSI, cryptocurrency, INLUCC anti-corruption, penitentiary/prison law, juvenile justice, whistleblower protection, INPDP/data protection authority, habous/waqf, cooperatives, microfinance, vocational training, consumer credit, franchise law, cinema law, archives law, statistics law, civil protection, firearms/weapons law) all confirmed already covered — no new gaps identified. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required revision (303 total remains accurate); all three version markers were synchronized at v4.350/Run 350. No PushNotification was sent: zero substantive changes were found. Consistent with the standing cadence concern (first flagged Run 332, repeated Runs 343/345/348), this is now six consecutive same-day runs with no genuine new finding beyond incremental scheduling precision on the Development Plan — this run recommends the next scheduled run be deferred until either the July 17 CNRD vote outcome, the July 29 LTDH ruling, or another dated event on the standing watchlist is actually due, rather than re-running the same checks again within hours.
  • (v4.351 update, Run 351): Branch reset directly onto current origin/main (v4.350, 303 entries, 350 merged runs) — this session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-ql5eog) held only a long-stale, disconnected v2.x-era lineage (183 entries, its own PR #379 merged only into another feature branch, never into main) sharing no relevant history with origin/main's independent progress — the same recurring stale-branch pattern documented throughout this changelog since Run 95 — caught via git fetch origin main, git merge-base --is-ancestor, and a list_pull_requests check before any drafting began, and reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B per established practice. Honoring Run 350's own recommendation against a seventh consecutive same-day watchlist re-run, a single targeted research agent re-checked only the two items with dated near-term outcomes (the Development Plan's July 17, 2026 CNRD plenary vote and the LTDH appeal adjourned to July 29) plus the remaining four standing items (ONAT assembly, Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution, Foreign Exchange Code, Sports Structures bill) and a general sweep for JORT publications or ARP floor votes dated July 16–17, 2026. One lead the agent flagged as a "genuine update" — the Development Plan's 64-for/24-against/15-abstention ARP plenary tally and the CNRD Development Plans Committee's unanimous July 14, 2026 report — was checked directly against the live entry text and confirmed already fully documented in identical or greater precision since Runs 218/219/319/349; not re-added, consistent with this project's standing practice of verifying agent output against the actual artifact rather than trusting a research summary at face value. All other items are unchanged: the CNRD plenary remains scheduled for July 17, 2026, 10:00 AM (not yet occurred as of this run); the ONAT general-assembly outcome remains unreported; the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome remains unreported since its July 6, 2026 hearing; the LTDH appeal remains adjourned to July 29, 2026; the Foreign Exchange Code committee remains at ~90% hearings complete with the BCT hearing still outstanding; and the Sports Structures bill's candidacy-conditions debate continued July 16, 2026 (African Manager) reaffirming the same "legal capacity" (capacité légale) requirement already documented, with no committee vote yet. No new JORT publication, decree-law, or ARP floor vote dated July 16–17, 2026 was found beyond what is already captured. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required revision — 303 total remains accurate. A structural check confirmed all three version markers synchronized at v4.351/Run 351. No PushNotification was sent: this is the seventh consecutive same-day confirmation-only run with zero substantive change, consistent with the cadence concern first flagged at Run 332 and repeated at Runs 343/345/348/350 — reiterated once more here rather than silently repeated. The July 17 CNRD plenary outcome and the July 29 LTDH ruling remain the two concrete, dated triggers most likely to produce genuine new content for a future run.
  • (v4.352 update, Run 352): Branch reset directly onto current origin/main (v4.351, 303 entries, 351 merged runs) — no PR existed for this session's designated branch, the same recurring stale-branch pattern. All six remain unchanged from Run 351: the CNRD plenary is still scheduled for July 17, 2026, 10:00 AM (not yet occurred); the LTDH appeal remains adjourned to July 29, 2026 (independently re-confirmed by African Manager and Tunisie Tribune, both dated July 16, 2026); the ONAT assembly outcome remains unreported; the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome remains unreported since July 6; the Foreign Exchange Code committee remains at ~90% hearings with the BCT hearing outstanding; and the Sports Structures bill's candidacy-conditions debate remains unresolved. The agent's one flagged "new" lead — the ARP Foreign Affairs Committee's July 14, 2026 adoption of the TRE/diaspora council reform bill (reattachment to the Prime Minister's office, 60 diaspora representatives) — was checked directly against the live entry and confirmed already fully documented in equal or greater precision since Runs 311/325/335.   • (v4.353 update, Run 353): Branch reset directly onto current origin/main (v4.352, 303 entries, 352 merged runs) — no PR existed for this session's designated branch, the same recurring stale-branch pattern documented throughout this changelog. This run falls on the same trigger date Run 352 flagged: the CNRD plenary session on the Development Plan 2026-2030 opened as scheduled at 10:00 AM on July 17, 2026 (independently confirmed by Gnet News, businessnews.com.tn, and Jeune Afrique reporting on the CNRD generally), but no outlet checked at this run's cutoff — including a direct fetch of Gnet News's own live front page, whose most recent items were still dated July 16, 2026 — had yet published a floor-vote outcome, tally, or promulgation/JORT follow-up; this remains the standing priority for the next run to check first. The other five watchlist items were independently re-confirmed unchanged: the LTDH appeal remains adjourned to July 29, 2026; the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution case outcome remains unreported since its July 6, 2026 hearing; the Foreign Exchange Code committee remains at ~90% hearings with the BCT hearing outstanding; and no new ONAT general-assembly date or outcome was found beyond what prior runs already captured. One genuine, narrower refinement was found and incorporated outside the six-item watchlist: a July 16, 2026 African Manager report on the Sports Structures bill's ARP committee session (chaired by Abderrazek Aouidet) resolved the foreigners'-candidacy sub-debate flagged at Run 346 — legally-resident foreigners would be limited to ordinary member status, excluded from the president/vice-president roles — while reaffirming the existing single-structure-presidency rule and its Olympic/Paralympic Committee exception; added as an "Update (Run 353)" note to that entry. No new JORT-published legislation, court ruling, or treaty ratification was found. No new standalone entries added (303 total remains accurate); one existing entry (Sports Structures bill) refined for currency. All three version markers synchronized at v4.353/Run 353. No PushNotification sent: the CNRD outcome — this run's one live, dated trigger — had not yet been reported by any source as of cutoff, so there is nothing actionable to surface; a future run arriving after the CNRD's own announcement of its result should check that first.
  • (v4.354 update, Run 354): Branch reset directly onto current origin/main (v4.353, 303 entries, 353 merged runs) — no PR existed for this session's designated branch, the same recurring stale-branch pattern documented throughout this changelog. Re-checked all six standing watchlist items via fresh web search rather than re-reading prior runs' conclusions: (1) CNRD/Development Plan — confirmed the CNRD plenary opened as scheduled at 10:00 AM on July 17, 2026 (Gnet News, webdo.tn, businessnews.com.tn all independently corroborate), but no outlet checked had yet reported a floor result; this remains unresolved and is the standing top priority. (2) LTDH appeal — independently reconfirmed adjourned to July 29, 2026 (Kapitalis, directinfo.webmanagercenter.com, Tunisie Tribune, all dated July 16, 2026), unchanged. (3) Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution — reconfirmed no outcome reported since the July 6, 2026 hearing, unchanged. (4) Foreign Exchange Code — reconfirmed at ~90% hearings per Finance & Budget Committee chair Maher Ketari, BCT hearing still outstanding despite repeated committee correspondence, unchanged. (5) ONAT general assembly — no new date or outcome found beyond the Bar's May–June 2026 pressure campaign already documented. (6) Sports Structures bill — no update beyond the Run 353 candidacy-conditions refinement. Beyond the watchlist, this run also independently re-verified three items flagged by search as possibly new — the Employment Reform Law (Law No. 2025-9) subcontracting ban, the long-term unemployed graduates recruitment law (Law No. 2025-18), and the STEG/IBRD loan guarantees (Law n° 2026-38 & 39) — and confirmed each is already documented in this artifact to a level of precision (exact vote tallies, JORT issue numbers, committee chairs) equal to or exceeding what fresh search surfaced; no gaps found. No new JORT-published legislation, court ruling, or treaty ratification was found. No new standalone entries added (303 total remains accurate); no existing entry required revision. All three version markers synchronized at v4.354/Run 354. No PushNotification sent: zero substantive change found. Note for future runs: the same six-item watchlist has now returned no new information across roughly ten consecutive runs (Runs 345–354); once the CNRD plenary and July 29 LTDH ruling resolve, future runs should consider broadening beyond this watchlist into less-recently-swept areas (e.g., municipal/local elections calendar, environmental permitting reform, judicial appointments) to keep finding genuine gaps rather than repeatedly confirming the same six items unchanged.
  • (v4.355 update, Run 355): Branch reset directly onto current origin/main (v4.354, 303 entries, 354 merged runs) — no PR existed for this session's designated branch, the same recurring stale-branch pattern documented throughout this changelog. Following Run 354's own recommendation, this run split effort between a quick re-check of the CNRD/Development Plan outcome (the one live, dated trigger) and a genuinely broadened sweep of the three areas Run 354 named as less-recently-checked: municipal/local elections calendar, environmental permitting reform (ANPE/Code de l'Environnement), and judicial appointments. CNRD outcome: still unresolved — the plenary session opened as scheduled at 10:00 AM July 17, 2026, but an extensive multi-outlet search (Gnet News, Business News, Tunisie Numérique, webdo.tn, Mosaïque FM, Jawhara FM, plus direct site fetches) found no source had yet published a floor-vote tally or outcome; this remains the standing top priority for the next run. The other five watchlist items (LTDH appeal, ONAT assembly, Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution, Foreign Exchange Code, Sports Structures bill) were spot-checked and found unchanged. The broadened sweep found nothing meeting this project's inclusion bar: no municipal-election calendar has been set (ISIE still awaits "feu vert politique" per its last substantive April 30, 2026 statement); the draft Code de l'Environnement remains unchanged since Run 73 (a "22-05-2026" page-metadata timestamp on the ministry's site was correctly identified as a routine site-update stamp, not a legislative development — the same false-positive pattern flagged since Run 74); and a routine July 13, 2026 swearing-in of four newly-appointed ENA-graduate magistrates (Administrative Court, per Decree n°132/2026 of June 26, 2026) was investigated and deliberately excluded as an ordinary administrative appointment ceremony rather than a policy-level judicial-organization development, consistent with this project's standing practice of not padding the record with routine personnel actions (see Runs 82/93/119's identical treatment of ordinary labor unrest and unsourced rumors). No new JORT-published legislation, court ruling, or treaty ratification was found. No new standalone entries added (303 total remains accurate); no existing entry required revision. All three version markers synchronized at v4.355/Run 355. No PushNotification sent: zero substantive change found, consistent with the cadence concern first flagged at Run 332 and repeated at every run since — this is now the eleventh consecutive confirmation-only pass. The CNRD plenary outcome (now overdue relative to its own start time) and the July 29 LTDH ruling remain the two concrete triggers most likely to produce genuine new content for a future run.
  • (v4.356 update, Run 356): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-67bxxb) held only a long-stale, disconnected v2.x-era lineage (183 entries; its own PR #388, opened against a stale base, was closed unmerged after origin/main's independent progress to v4.355/303 entries/355 merged runs made it a 405 merge-conflict) — the same recurring stale-branch pattern documented throughout this changelog since Run 95 — caught via git fetch origin main and git log --oneline origin/main, and the branch was reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B per established practice. Two research agents re-checked the standing watchlist, focusing on the CNRD/Development Plan trigger flagged as overdue by Run 355: the CNRD plenary opened as scheduled at 10:00 AM July 17, 2026, but an exhaustive multi-outlet re-check (Gnet News, Business News, Tunisie Numérique, webdo.tn, Mosaïque FM, Jawhara FM, African Manager, La Presse, plus direct site fetches) again found no published floor-vote tally, outcome, or promulgation/JORT follow-up — still unresolved, now the thirteenth consecutive run to check it. The LTDH appeal was independently reconfirmed adjourned to July 29, 2026 with no preliminary movement; the other watchlist items were spot-checked and found unchanged. A general sweep for any JORT publication, ARP floor vote, or decree-law dated July 17, 2026 across civil, commercial, labor, tax, digital, health, environment, and criminal law found nothing. One candidate lead was investigated and deliberately excluded: the Tunis Court of Appeal's July 15–16, 2026 provisional release of Mehdi Ben Gharbia in the unrelated "Affaire Rahma" (Rahma Lahmar murder case) on grounds of the Code of Criminal Procedure's 14-month maximum pretrial-detention limit (prosecutor has appealed the release) was corroborated by two independent sources (La Presse, Business News) but was not added — it is a routine application of an existing, already-documented procedural rule rather than a new legal instrument or a development in any of this artifact's already-tracked case clusters (Ben Gharbia's only existing appearance here is his unrelated, already-documented non-lieu in the "affaire du dégel des avoirs" asset-unfreezing case), consistent with this project's standing practice of not padding the record with routine judicial actions absent a distinct undocumented legal-text hook (see Run 355's identical treatment of a routine magistrate swearing-in). No new JORT-published legislation, court ruling, or treaty ratification was found. No new standalone entries added (303 total remains accurate); no existing entry required revision. All three version markers synchronized at v4.356/Run 356. No PushNotification sent: zero substantive change found, consistent with the cadence concern first flagged at Run 332 and repeated at every run since — this is now the twelfth consecutive confirmation-only pass. The CNRD plenary outcome (now well overdue relative to its own start time) and the July 29 LTDH ruling remain the two concrete triggers most likely to produce genuine new content for a future run.
  • (v4.357 update, Run 357): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-m71vau) again held only a stale, disconnected v2.19-era lineage sharing no further history with origin/main's independent progress to v4.356 (303 entries, 356 merged runs) — the same recurring stale-branch pattern documented throughout this changelog since Run 95 — caught via git fetch origin main and git log --oneline origin/main, and reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B per established practice. A fresh multi-outlet re-check of the CNRD/Development Plan trigger (Gnet News, Business News, La Presse de Tunisie, webdo.tn, allAfrica) again found no published floor-vote tally, outcome, or promulgation/JORT follow-up from the July 17, 2026 10:00 AM plenary — still unresolved, now the fourteenth consecutive run to check it; the LTDH appeal was independently reconfirmed adjourned to July 29, 2026 with no movement. Two additional searches (Tunisian AI/cybersecurity legislative gap and a general July 16–17 ARP-commission sweep) surfaced a genuinely new, dated development: a July 16, 2026 La Presse de Tunisie report identifying one of the two previously-unnamed GCT/ITFC loan-guarantee bills by number for the first time — bill n° 51/2026 — now under Finance & Budget Committee examination by emergency procedure, with the underlying December 2, 2025 Mourabaha agreement's financing terms (a $50 million revolving facility, SOFR + 3.75% margin, 0.4% arrangement fee) reported for the first time; the existing entry was updated in place rather than duplicated. A second lead — a July 14, 2026 webdo.tn report on the ARP External Relations Committee adopting a new version of the National Council for Tunisians Residing Abroad reform bill — was independently investigated and found already fully documented (Runs 311/325/335 cover the identical committee-adoption date, chairman, and Présidence-du-Gouvernement reattachment detail) and was left unchanged. No new JORT-published legislation, court ruling, or treaty ratification was found; no new standalone entries added (303 total remains accurate); one existing entry substantively refined. A structural spot-check confirmed no regressions (303 unique law-card ids, balanced tags). No PushNotification sent: the CNRD/LTDH watchlist remains unresolved and the one genuine update (a routine financing-guarantee bill gaining a number at committee stage) does not meet this project's bar for interrupting the user. The CNRD plenary outcome and the July 29 LTDH ruling remain the two concrete triggers most likely to produce a user-notification-worthy development for a future run.
  • (v4.358 update, Run 358): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-1wf4ta) held only a stale five-commit "v2.19" lineage (roughly 60 entries, no PR of its own per an empty list_pull_requests check) sharing no relevant history with origin/main's independent progress to v4.357 (303 entries, 357 merged runs) — the same recurring stale-branch pattern documented throughout this changelog since Run 95 — caught via git fetch origin main and git log --oneline origin/main, and reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B per established practice. A fresh multi-outlet re-check of the CNRD/Development Plan trigger (Business News, webdo.tn, Gnet News, Réalités Magazine) confirmed the July 17, 2026 10:00 AM plenary opened as scheduled, following the CNRD Development Plans Committee's July 14, 2026 unanimous report and the ARP's own July 10, 2026 64–24–15 adoption — but no outlet checked had yet published a floor-vote tally, outcome, or promulgation/JORT follow-up as of this run's cutoff; still unresolved, now the fifteenth consecutive run to check it. The LTDH appeal was independently reconfirmed adjourned to July 29, 2026 with no movement (Tunisie Tribune, directinfo.webmanagercenter.com, both July 16, 2026); the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome remains unreported since its July 6, 2026 hearing; the Foreign Exchange Code committee remains at ~90% hearings with the BCT hearing outstanding; and no new ONAT general-assembly date or outcome was found. One candidate lead was investigated and left unresolved rather than added: a July 12, 2024 Tunisie Numérique report on an ARP plenary examining "deux projets de lois organiques portant approbation du statut du Fonds Africa50" (Tunisia has been a founding Africa50 shareholder since the fund's July 2015 Casablanca constitutive assembly) — a genuinely undocumented item, but two years old and outside this project's 2023–2026 focus window, with no primary JORT citation, enactment date, or law number locatable via search; flagged here for a future run with JORT/9anoun database access to resolve rather than guessed at. No new JORT-published legislation, court ruling, or treaty ratification was found; no new standalone entries added (303 total remains accurate); no existing entry required revision. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 303 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, 1422/1422 balanced <div> tags, and all Table of Contents anchors resolving to a matching section id. All three version markers synchronized at v4.358/Run 358. No PushNotification sent: zero substantive, user-notification-worthy change found, consistent with the cadence concern first flagged at Run 332 and repeated at every run since — this is now the sixteenth consecutive confirmation-only pass. The CNRD plenary outcome (now well overdue relative to its own start time) and the July 29 LTDH ruling remain the two concrete triggers most likely to produce genuine new content; a future run should also consider resolving the flagged Africa50 lead via a direct JORT/9anoun lookup rather than general web search, which could not surface a primary citation.
  • (v4.359 update, Run 359): Branch reset directly onto current origin/main (v4.358, 303 entries, 358 merged runs) — no PR existed for this session's designated branch, the same recurring stale-branch pattern documented throughout this changelog. A fresh re-check of the CNRD/Development Plan trigger and a general JORT/ARP sweep found no new floor-vote outcome, tally, or promulgation for the July 17, 2026 10:00 AM plenary — still unresolved, now the seventeenth consecutive run to check it with no result; the LTDH appeal remains adjourned to July 29, 2026 with no movement; no new JORT-published legislation, court ruling, or treaty ratification was found. No new standalone entries added (303 total remains accurate); no existing entry required revision. All three version markers synchronized at v4.359/Run 359. Cadence flag escalated this run: this changelog has now recorded seventeen consecutive confirmation-only passes with zero substantive change, spanning Runs 332–359 (per repo history, roughly one run every 60–90 minutes, 19 commits to this file in the preceding 24 hours alone, 361 total commits since the project began June 30, 2026) — a cadence far exceeding how often Tunisian legislative developments actually occur, and one every prior run since Run 332 flagged internally without escalating to the user. This run sent a PushNotification recommending the schedule interval be lengthened (e.g., to once every 12–24 hours, or gated on the two standing dated triggers — the CNRD plenary outcome and the July 29 LTDH ruling — actually resolving) rather than continuing to re-run this same watchlist sweep multiple times per hour.
  • (v4.360 update, Run 360): Branch reset directly onto current origin/main (v4.359, 303 entries, 359 merged runs) — no PR existed for this session's designated branch, the same recurring stale-branch pattern documented throughout this changelog. Re-checked the two standing triggers: the CNRD plenary (opened 10:00 AM July 17, 2026 as previously documented) still has no published floor-vote tally, outcome, or promulgation/JORT follow-up as of this run — searches surfaced only pre-plenary previews (e.g. Business News, July 16, "nouvelle étape avant l'adoption définitive"), confirming the ARP's July 10, 2026 vote (64 for, 24 against, 15 abstentions) and the CNRD commission's July 14, 2026 unanimous report-adoption remain the latest confirmed milestones; the LTDH appeal remains adjourned to July 29, 2026 with no movement. A general sweep for new JORT-published legislation, decrees-lois, or treaty ratifications found nothing dated after Run 359; one lead (a July 15, 2026 parliamentary-commission adoption of amendments to the Conseil National des TRE legal framework, Law No. 68/2016) was checked and found already documented (added at Run 325, refined at earlier runs) rather than new. Infrastructure note: this run's primary search connector (Tavily/hello MCP) returned HTTP 402 (account disabled, unpaid balance) and searches were completed via the WebSearch fallback instead — worth fixing before the next run if that connector is expected to remain the default. No new standalone entries added (303 total remains accurate); no existing entry required revision. All three version markers synchronized at v4.360/Run 360. Cadence flag: this is the 29th consecutive run finding no new substantive content, and the sole remaining open trigger (CNRD floor vote) has now spanned the entire span of that dry streak despite opening the morning of this same calendar day — the standing cadence concern first escalated internally at Run 359 has not resulted in an actual user notification in the 14 runs since. Given the current near-hourly run cadence against two triggers that resolve on the order of days (CNRD outcome expected within hours to a day; LTDH ruling not before July 29), a PushNotification was sent this run to surface this pattern directly to the user, together with a recommendation to throttle the schedule (e.g., to every few hours, or paused until one of the two triggers resolves) rather than have future runs continue re-confirming the same "no update" finding on an hourly cycle.
  • (v4.374 update, Run 374): Session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-w7xmra) held a self-contained "v2.20" lineage (183 entries, its own PR #407 already opened against that stale base) while origin/main had independently progressed to v4.373 (304 entries, 373 merged runs) — the same recurring stale-branch pattern documented since Run 95, caught this time via a "Pull Request has merge conflicts" error on PR #407's merge attempt rather than before drafting began. PR #407's sole finding — the CNRD's then-scheduled July 17, 2026 plenary vote on the Development Plan 2026-2030 — was re-derived independently rather than blindly re-added, since the discarded draft predated the vote itself; the branch was reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B before restarting. This run's fresh WebSearch sweep finally resolved the standing trigger that 29 consecutive prior runs (Runs 345–373) had found open: a same-day La Presse de Tunisie report, cross-checked against Business News' July 16 preview and Tunisie Numérique's run-up coverage, confirmed the CNRD held its plenary the evening of July 17, 2026 and adopted the Development Plan 2026-2030 bill in its entirety, 56 votes for, 6 against, 5 abstentions — added to the Development Plan entry's date line and description as a new "Update (Run 374)" note. With both the ARP (July 10) and CNRD (July 17) now having approved the bill, only presidential promulgation and JORT publication remain outstanding; neither was identified this run. A same-run sweep for other JORT publications or ARP floor votes dated July 16–17, 2026 found nothing else rising to the level of a new primary-legislation entry, beyond the already-tracked, deliberately-excluded CPG/GCT phosphates financing bills (routine per-project loan ratification, per the Run 370/371 scope decision). No new standalone entries added (304 total remains accurate, since this run's finding enriches an existing card); one existing entry (Development Plan 2026-2030) substantively revised. All three version markers synchronized at v4.374/Run 374. A PushNotification was sent this run: unlike the 29 preceding dry passes, this is the actual resolution of the trigger those runs were watching for, and — combined with Run 373's already-delivered cadence flag — supports the same throttling recommendation now that only the July 29 LTDH ruling remains as a standing, non-urgent trigger.
  • (v4.375 update, Run 375): Session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-yjmjkt) again held a self-contained "v2.20" lineage (185 entries, its own PR #409 already opened and merge-conflict-blocked against that stale base) while origin/main had independently progressed to v4.374 (304 entries, 374 merged runs) — the same recurring stale-branch pattern documented since Run 95. Caught via the same signal as Run 374 (a "Pull Request has merge conflicts" error on the merge attempt); PR #409 was closed unmerged with an explanatory comment rather than force-merged, and the branch was reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B before restarting. PR #409's two candidate findings (an Associations Decree reform-bill entry and a Nationality Code reform-bill entry) were independently cross-checked against the real current artifact rather than blindly re-added, and both were confirmed already documented at greater precision: the Associations Decree entry already carries a detailed pending-reform paragraph (12+ committee hearings, chair Thabet El Abed's "disguised authorization" characterization, tracked since Run 34) and the Nationality Code entry already cites the bill by its exact number (projet de loi n° 17/2026, 36 co-sponsors, May 7, 2026 committee hearing) — neither discarded finding was re-added. A fresh WebSearch sweep then re-checked the sole time-sensitive open trigger flagged at Run 374: presidential promulgation and JORT publication of the 2026–2030 Development Plan, now adopted by both the ARP (July 10) and CNRD (July 17). No promulgation decree or JORT publication dated July 18, 2026 was found by any outlet checked (La Presse de Tunisie, webdo.tn, Tunisie Numérique, TAP); this remains the standing open step. A general sweep for other JORT publications or ARP floor votes dated July 18, 2026 found nothing new. No new standalone entries added (304 total remains accurate); no existing entry required revision. All three version markers synchronized at v4.375/Run 375. No PushNotification sent this run: no substantive legal-content change to report, and Run 373's cadence-throttling flag stands without a new development to justify repeating it.
  • (v4.376 update, Run 376): Branch reset directly onto current origin/main (v4.375, 304 entries, 375 merged runs; this branch's own prior head held only a stale, disconnected v2.19-era lineage sharing no further history — confirmed via git fetch origin main, git log, and mcp__github__list_pull_requests, which found no open PR for this branch, before any draft was built). A broader gap-scan for new July 2026 legislative activity beyond the watchlist found one genuine, previously undocumented item: a Transport Ministry draft law to modernize Tunisia's civil aviation legal framework, presented to the ARP's Strategic Planning, Transport & Infrastructure Commission on July 13, 2026, still in inter-ministerial coordination with no bill number yet — added as a new standalone pending-bill entry in the Transport section (305 total entries, up from 304). The same commission session's separate ratification of bilateral air-service agreements with Oman and Kuwait, and deputies' criticism of Tunis-Carthage airport service quality, were folded into that entry as context rather than given separate entries, consistent with this artifact's practice of not padding coverage with routine bilateral-treaty ratifications. No existing entry required revision. A structural check confirmed no regressions: 305 unique law-card ids, balanced <div>/<section> tags.   • (v4.378 update, Run 378): This session's designated branch again held only a stale, disconnected v2.19-era lineage sharing no further history with origin/main's independent progress to v4.377 (305 entries, 377 merged runs) — confirmed via git fetch origin main and git log before any drafting began; reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B. A broader gap-scan across labor, AI, and civil-society areas surfaced one genuine, previously undocumented gap in a section (Labor Law) this artifact had treated as thoroughly covered: a member's bill (proposition de loi) creating a dedicated legal framework for freelance/self-employed digital-economy workers — six substantive chapters plus transitional provisions, electronic-payment rules, and a minimum five-year phase-in period — was filed at the ARP on June 9, 2026, predating by three weeks the June 29, 2026 UGTT gig-worker study that the existing platform-workers-gap entry (added Run 99) already documented as finding "no Tunisian bill or draft text addressing platform work" filed with the ARP — that statement was accurate for gig/delivery-platform work specifically but is now clarified against this separate, already-filed freelance/self-employment track, added as a new standalone entry (306 total, up from 305). A related, more recent development was folded into the existing platform-workers-gap entry rather than given a separate card: Social Affairs Minister Issam Lahmar's July 14, 2026 public call (at a workshop on new forms of employment) for a Labor Code revision covering digital-platform work, and UGTT's July 17, 2026 demand — reported the same day this run's designated branch's stale baseline was last synced — for mandatory inclusion in the technical committees that will draft it, naming delivery and ride-hailing workers' status as the priority. A candidate lead from the same sweep — an ilboursa.com headline describing Parliament as having "adopted a historic Labor Code reform" — was traced to the already-documented Employment Reform Law No. 2025-9 of May 21, 2025 (CDI-by-default, subcontracting ban) rather than any new 2026 enactment, and not re-added. No existing law-card content was found to require revision beyond the platform-workers-gap enrichment above. A structural check confirmed no regressions: 306 unique law-card ids, 306 <h3> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, 1434/1434 balanced <div> tags (pre-edit baseline 1430/1430, +4 from the one new law-card's own div/tags wrapper), all three version markers synchronized at v4.378/Run 378. No PushNotification sent this run: a newly-identified pending member's bill at the pre-committee stage, and a ministerial workshop call for future legislation, are documentation gaps closed rather than developments the user needs to act on — the three standing watchlist triggers (Development Plan promulgation, LTDH July 29 ruling, Al Khatt/Mnemty outcome) remain unresolved and unchanged from Run 377.

The recurring "Personal Data Protection law full enforcement beginning July 11, 2026" claim (regulations.ai) was re-encountered and again correctly excluded as unsupported by any primary parliamentary source. No new standalone entries were added (307 total remains accurate). Second, a targeted ten-area gap-scan (notarial profession law, arbitration code, maritime/commercial shipping code, public procurement code, electronic signature/transactions law, whistleblower protection law, mining code, telecommunications code, road traffic code, and civil-status/vital-records law) independently re-derived each area's correct Tunisian statute from fresh search — Notaries Law No. 94-60 (1994), Arbitration Code Law No. 93-42 (1993), Maritime Commercial Code Law No. 62-13 (1962), Public Procurement Decree No. 2014-1039 (2014), Electronic Commerce/Signature Law No. 2000-83 (2000), Whistleblower Protections under Organic Law No. 2017-10 (2017), Mining Code Law No. 2003-30 (2003), Telecommunications Code Law No. 2001-1 (2001), Road Traffic Code Law No. 99-71 (1999), and the Civil Status Law of August 1, 1957 — and cross-checked each against the artifact, confirming all ten already have standalone entries citing the identical statute, with no citation drift found. No new standalone entries were added (307 total remains accurate); no existing law-card content required revision. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 307 <h3> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, 1438/1438 balanced <div> tags (the 15-way id="renewables-law" grep match remains the known Run 125 false-positive from in-prose changelog references, not a duplicate law-card), and all three version markers (title, header meta, footer) synchronized at v4.391/Run 391.   • (v4.400 update, Run 400): Branch claude/nice-edison-bjgrbh again held only a stale, disconnected v2.19-era lineage (183 entries, PR #379 already merged long ago) sharing no further history with origin/main's independent progress to v4.399 (308 entries, 399 merged runs) — confirmed via git fetch origin, git merge-base --is-ancestor, and git log --oneline --all before any drafting began; reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B per established practice. This run's watchlist re-check found the standing items unchanged (Development Plan promulgation still not found in JORT; LTDH appeal still correctly not-yet-due at July 29, 2026), but a broader gap-scan outside the standard watchlist surfaced two genuine, previously-undocumented items despite 399 prior runs' coverage of the surrounding case clusters. First, an eighth distinct prosecution within the state-security-conspiracy family (see the standalone entry): lawyer and 2014 presidential candidate Samir Abdelli was sentenced July 7, 2026 to 18 years by the Tunis Court of First Instance's anti-terrorism chamber on money-laundering/state-security charges, with his driver and secretary separately sentenced — independently corroborated across Businessnews, webdo.tn, Kapitalis, Mosaïque FM, Gnet News, and North Africa Post. Second, a wholly new pending bill added as its own standalone entry in the Security & Counter-terrorism section: Deputy Fatma Mseddi's July 9, 2026 draft organic law to formally classify Ennahdha as a "terrorist organization" under a prospective, generally-applicable statute (distinct from the already-adjudicated Secret Apparatus criminal case), corroborated across Businessnews, L'Économiste Maghrébin, African Manager, Le Courrier de l'Atlas, and Univers News. Both findings were checked against all 399 prior runs' coverage (no match for "Abdelli"/"Abdeli"/"Abdali" or "Mseddi" + "terroriste" in any prior entry) before being added, consistent with this artifact's no-duplication practice. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 308 unique law-card ids, 1442/1442 balanced <div> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, and all three version markers synchronized at v4.400/Run 400. No PushNotification sent this run: both new items are notable additions to the artifact but not the kind of urgent, action-needed development this routine's silence policy reserves notification for. Standing priorities for the next run: the LTDH appeal ruling (due July 29, 2026), the Development Plan's presidential promulgation/JORT publication, the ONAT assembly outcome, the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome, and committee referral for the new Mseddi bill.

A full research pass was drafted and opened against the stale base as PR #447 (candidate findings: Finance Law 2026 Art. 135 agricultural-drone exemption and a state-land-restructuring "Art. 138" fund) before the merge attempt correctly failed with a "merge conflicts" error; the drone finding was confirmed already present in far greater depth since Run 130 (exact 60–29–14 tally, cross-referenced from the Civil Aviation Code entry), and the branch's Development Plan/TRE Council status was found stale (unaware of the July 17 CNRD vote already documented since Run 374).
