Rather than assert either unverified article number, both were preserved with an explicit "disputed, unresolved pending primary JORT text" caveat, added to the Finance Law 2026 entry — consistent with this project's established practice for conflicting-source findings (e.g., the Run 13 conjunctural-contribution caveat in the same entry). A fresh, focused re-check of the standing watchlist found no other changes: the Development Plan still shows no presidential promulgation or JORT publication; the LTDH appeal remains correctly not-yet-due (July 29, 2026); the Mseddi bill remains without a confirmed committee hearing date; and a July 8, 2026 Human Rights Watch reference to a 21-defendant, 12–35-year sentencing was checked and confirmed to describe the already-documented Ennahda "Conspiracy 2" first-instance verdict of July 8, 2025, not a new 2026 event — a potential false-positive correctly ruled out. No new standalone entries were added (308 total remains accurate; this run enriched one existing entry). A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 308 unique law-card ids, 1442/1442 balanced <div> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, and all three version markers synchronized at v4.407/Run 407. This time the discrepancy was not caught before drafting: a full research pass was built and opened as PR #459 against the stale base, with three candidate findings — the Development Plan's July 17 CNRD adoption, the Finance Law 2026 Art. 15 wage-setting-by-decree mechanism, and Circular No. 11 of August 11, 2025 ending trade-union secondment — before an attempted merge correctly failed with a "merge conflicts" error. Two of the three findings were confirmed already present in the real artifact, in more precise and better-corroborated form: the Development Plan entry already carried the identical 56–6–5 CNRD tally, and the wage-decrees-2026 entry already documented Art. 15 in far greater depth (the full seven-decree breakdown vs. the stale draft's single-paragraph summary). PR #459 was closed unmerged with an explanatory comment, and the branch was reset directly onto current origin/main (v4.418, 308 entries, 418 merged runs) via git checkout -B per established practice. The third finding, Circular No. 11 of the Head of Government (August 11, 2025) ending trade-union secondment ("détachement syndical"), was independently re-verified as a genuine gap — corroborated via two independent French-language reports (webdo.tn and WebManagerCenter, both dated August 14, 2025, agreeing on the August 11 effective date) — and added as a new standalone Labor Law entry, cross-referenced from the wage-decrees-2026 entry as a contemporaneous but legally distinct measure narrowing UGTT's institutional role. A fresh, independent verification pass then re-checked the three highest-priority standing-watchlist items via new WebSearch queries: the Development Plan's presidential promulgation/JORT publication — still not found in any outlet checked (La Presse de Tunisie, L'Économiste Maghrébin, allAfrica); and the LTDH appeal — reconfirmed via Kapitalis, Tunisie Tribune, African Manager, and directinfo.webmanagercenter.com as adjourned to July 29, 2026 pending a response to the state-litigation attorney general's report, correctly not yet due. One new standalone entry added (309 total). Flagged separately to the user (outside this changelog) as an operational concern: the stale-branch/wasted-draft pattern has now recurred across at least 8 documented runs, alongside evidence that this task is being triggered far more frequently than a single daily/periodic schedule would produce (418 runs since late June 2026, with multiple runs per hour on some days) — worth the user's attention independent of this run's legal-content findings. Standing priorities for the next run are unchanged: the LTDH appeal ruling (due July 29, 2026), the Development Plan's presidential promulgation/JORT publication, the ONAT assembly outcome, the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome, the Mseddi bill's committee referral, and the identity of the two remaining CPG/GCT-batch bills.

One candidate lead was investigated and rejected: an Anadolu Agency (aa.com.tr) headline referencing a Kaïs Saïed decree-law amending the ISIE organic law was fetched directly and dated April 22, 2022 — the well-known ISIE-composition decree-law (Decree-Law No. 2022-32, presidential appointment of all seven members) already documented under Electoral Law since early runs, not a July 2026 development; a search-snippet reference to Mauritanian Council of Ministers HIV/AIDS, petroleum-pricing, and mining-permit decrees was also correctly recognized (per the Run 416/418 precedent) as a non-Tunisian false lead and discarded without being added. The July 15, 2026 Haythem El Mekki Article 86 appeal conviction and the July 21, 2026 Belloumi/Boughdiri opposition hearing (one day out) were independently re-checked and found already fully documented (Runs 338 and 393 respectively) in more depth than any source consulted this run. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required substantive revision (309 total remains accurate). No PushNotification sent this run: a same-day re-confirmation of an unchanged watchlist, with two candidate leads investigated and correctly rejected, is not a development requiring the user's attention.   • (v4.423 update, Run 423): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-rn8nqe) again held only a stale, disconnected v2.19-era lineage sharing no history with origin/main's independent progress, and no PR of its own (confirmed via list_pull_requests) — caught via git fetch origin main and a log comparison before any drafting began, and the branch was reset directly onto current origin/main (v4.422, 309 entries, 422 merged runs) via git checkout -B, avoiding the wasted-draft/PR-conflict pattern that recurred at Runs 417, 419, 420, and 422. A dedicated research agent re-checked the full eight-item standing watchlist and ran a fresh gap-scan. One genuine watchlist resolution: the CPG/GCT loan-guarantee batch's two previously-unidentified bills are now named — per African Manager's July 15, 2026 report, bill (4) is an "avenant modificatif n°2" to the BCT/Afreximbank state-financing loan (see the Afreximbank entry) and bill (5) is the formal closure of the FY2023 state budget accounts — closing a gap open since Run 320. The other seven watchlist items (Development Plan promulgation/JORT publication, ONAT assembly outcome, Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution, LTDH appeal, Foreign Exchange Code committee status, Mseddi bill referral, Personal Data Protection bill) were independently reconfirmed unchanged; a secondary-source claim that the Mseddi bill had reached parliamentary examination (an unresolvable aggregator headline, "INFO.FR," July 18, 2026) could not be corroborated against any primary Tunisian outlet and was correctly withheld rather than added. The gap-scan's other four candidate leads (digital-platform/gig-economy worker regulation, the Associations Decree reform bill's 45-article/8-chapter structure and Article 26 foreign-funding-consent mechanism, the cinema/audiovisual investment-fund bill, and the animal-welfare bill) were each cross-checked directly against the live document rather than the agent's own assessment and found already substantively captured — respectively in the Digital Platform Workers entry (through Run 378), the existing NGO Reform entry (identical 45-article/8-chapter/Article 26 detail already present), the Cinema and Cultural Industries entry, and the Animal Protection entry — none re-added as if new. Three existing entries were enriched with genuinely new corroborated detail not previously captured: the Status of the Artist entry now cites the bill's 76-1-0 plenary vote tally, 45-article length, and minimum-Tunisian-content broadcast-quota provision; the Animal Protection entry now notes a May 6, 2026 report describing the bill under review as a 12-title/111-article "one health" structuring text with mandatory living-condition standards and companion-animal microchipping, flagged as an additional, not-yet-reconciled data point given the entry's already-documented bill-numbering ambiguity; and the Judicial Statute entry gained a new false-positive-trap note distinguishing the still-unresolved July 11, 2026 ONAT assembly from a separate, earlier May 1, 2026 extraordinary assembly that voted the general strike already documented above. No new standalone entries were added (309 total remains accurate); five existing entries were revised for currency. A structural audit found no regressions: 309 unique law-card ids, all internal anchor links (including the three newly added this run) resolving to a matching id, and all three version markers synchronized at v4.423/Run 423. No PushNotification sent for legal content this run — the CPG/GCT resolution and entry enrichments are incremental refinements of already-substantially-documented material, not a new development requiring the user's immediate attention; the standing operational concern (this task's far-above-periodic firing rate, flagged and left unaddressed across Runs 419–422) was not re-flagged again by push notification this run since repeating an already-quadruply-flagged, unaddressed concern with no new information adds no value on its own — it is, however, repeated once more in this changelog entry for the record, since the underlying condition (this run again began from a stale, disconnected branch snapshot rather than current main) recurred unchanged. Standing priorities for the next run: the LTDH appeal ruling (due July 29, 2026), the Development Plan's presidential promulgation/JORT publication, the ONAT assembly outcome, the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome, and the Mseddi bill's committee referral.

  • (v4.425 update, Run 425): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-4sukkh) again held only a stale, disconnected v2.20-era lineage (183 entries, last touched July 20, 2026 but rooted in the same pre-v2.0 history as Runs 417–424) sharing no history with origin/main's independent progress — caught via git fetch origin main and a log comparison before any drafting began, and the branch was reset directly onto current origin/main (v4.424, 309 entries, 424 merged runs) via git checkout -B, avoiding the wasted-draft/PR-conflict pattern. Tavily search/extract tools failed this run with a new error (HTTP 402 "account currently disabled... unpaid pay-as-you-go balance," distinct from the long-standing generic 402 "account disabled" seen since Run 68 — the same underlying tool, now specifically confirmed billing-related), so this run relied on WebSearch and WebFetch instead. A fresh sweep re-checked the standing watchlist and found no change on any item: the Development Plan's presidential promulgation/JORT publication — a July 19, 2026 L'Économiste Maghrébin article ("Ça y est, le Plan de développement 2026-2030 définitivement adopté") was fetched directly and confirmed to describe only the July 17 CNRD vote, with no mention of presidential promulgation or JORT publication despite the "définitivement adopté" headline; the ONAT July 11, 2026 assembly outcome — still unreported by any outlet found, now 64+ consecutive runs unresolved; the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution proceedings — the July 6, 2026 hearing's own report (webmanagercenter, fetched directly) confirms only the postponement to July 6, with no ruling reported since; the Mseddi/Ennahdha bill — reconfirmed filed July 9, 2026 (six articles), still no confirmed committee hearing date; and the Foreign Exchange Code — reconfirmed at the same ~90%-hearings-complete status (chair Maher Ktari), still awaiting Central Bank consultation before final adoption. No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication was identified. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required revision (309 total remains accurate) — a pure confirmation run. Operational concern re-escalated: a full audit of origin/main's commit history shows this task has run at a sustained rate of roughly 30–44 commits (≈15–22 runs) per day, every day, from its June 30, 2026 start through today — 424 runs in 20 days, i.e. an average of one run every ~68 minutes around the clock, with zero slow days. This is not a transient spike: it is the constant behavior of the entire three-week history, and it has now been flagged internally in this changelog at Runs 419, 421, 422, 423, and 424 without any visible change in firing rate. Combined with the fact that a large fraction of these runs (at least 10 documented: 417, 419, 420, 422, 424, 425, plus five earlier) begin from a stale/disconnected branch snapshot rather than current main and must self-correct via git checkout -B before any real work happens, each run is burning a full research-and-drafting cycle — and, per this run's finding, potentially incurring Tavily billing charges on top of it — for a legal-content watchlist that has not materially changed in at least the last 10 runs. A PushNotification is being sent this run specifically to surface this operational issue, since prior escalations appear not to have resulted in a corrected schedule.

No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication was identified. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required revision beyond the Development Plan's corroborating citation (309 total remains accurate) — a pure confirmation run. One genuine, well-corroborated gap was found and closed: Proposition de loi n° 026/2023, a pending bill amending Law No. 2000-73 on private higher education (affecting all 85 private higher-education institutions) — requiring 30% of teaching staff to be permanent doctorate-holders on public-sector-equivalent pay and teaching-hour terms, with a two-year compliance deadline and enrollment-ban sanctions for non-compliance — had completed nine Education & Sports Commission hearings since October 2023 and, per Tunisie Numérique (June 18, 2026), had its final report transmitted to the ARP Bureau awaiting plenary scheduling; cross-checked against Managers.tn's March 10, 2025 committee-hearing report, which independently confirmed the same bill number, amended-law citation, and substantive terms. This is a distinct bill from the already-documented public-university-president-appointment reform on the same Higher Education Law entry, which now cross-references it. Added as a new standalone Private Higher Education Reform Bill entry (310 total, up from 309). No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication was identified. No PushNotification sent: an addition to the pending-bill watchlist, not a new enactment or resolution of a standing watch-item, does not meet the bar for the user's attention on its own. Standing priorities for the next run remain: the LTDH appeal ruling (due July 29, 2026), the Development Plan's presidential promulgation/JORT publication, the ONAT assembly outcome, the Mseddi bill's committee referral, the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome, and now also the private higher-education bill's plenary scheduling.

  • (v4.446 update, Run 446): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-71s1qv) again held only a long-stale, disconnected v2.20-era checkout (July 20, 2026 vintage, 2,219 lines, 183 entries, with one further commit made against that stale base before the discrepancy was caught) sharing no history with origin/main's independent progress to v4.445 (310 entries, 445 merged runs) — the same recurring failure mode documented since Run 95. The stale commit was discarded in full (never pushed) and the branch reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B before any further work, per established practice. A WebSearch/WebFetch sweep resolved this artifact's single longest-standing open item: the Development Plan 2026–2030's presidential promulgation, unconfirmed across roughly 227 runs since Run 218/219 first recorded the ARP's July 10, 2026 floor vote. President Kaïs Saïed received the Prime Minister, the Minister of Economy and Planning, and the Central Bank Governor at Carthage Palace on July 20, 2026 specifically to discuss implementation of the plan's now-promulgated approval law; independently confirmed via Réalités Magazine ("s'est félicité...de la promulgation de la loi...") and webdo ("la loi de ratification a été promulguée"), two independently-bylined reports of the same meeting, satisfying the two-independent-source rule every prior run's dedicated searches had failed to clear. A dedicated follow-up search for the specific JORT issue number, promulgation date, or presidential decree number came up empty (jort.tn's homepage listing showed only unrelated "Annonces Légales" issues N°084-086; a direct WebFetch of the businessnews.com.tn article covering the same meeting returned HTTP 503 on repeated attempts) — this citation gap is preserved as the entry's sole remaining open item rather than blocking recognition of the promulgation itself. Added as an in-place Update (Run 446) to the existing entry's status line and description, consistent with this artifact's practice of tracking status changes without relocating cards between sections. The remaining watchlist was independently re-checked and found unchanged: the LTDH appeal — still adjourned to July 29, 2026, not yet due; the Mseddi/Ennahdha bill — still no confirmed committee referral since its July 9, 2026 filing; and the Foreign Exchange Code reform — still under Finance and Budget Commission review, no floor vote found. A lateral gap-check confirmed the State Domains Code & Collective Land Decree-Law entry (Minister Hedhili's July 14, 2026 southern collective-land announcement) already fully captures a candidate lead this run's own research had independently turned up — not re-added. No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication beyond the Development Plan promulgation was identified. No new standalone entries were added (310 total remains accurate, since the sole finding enriches an existing card); one existing entry substantively revised. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 310 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, 310 <h3> tags, 1,450/1,450 balanced <div> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, and all three version markers synchronized at v4.446/Run 446. PushNotification sent this run: resolution of the artifact's single most persistently-tracked watchlist item across roughly 227 runs clears the bar for the user's attention that the prior several confirmation-only runs did not meet. Standing priorities for the next run: the exact JORT issue/decree-number citation for the Development Plan's promulgation, the LTDH appeal ruling (due July 29, 2026), the ONAT assembly outcome, the Mseddi bill's committee referral, the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome (open since July 6), the private higher-education bill's plenary scheduling, the telework/platform-economy bills' formal ARP filing, the diaspora-council bill's ARP Bureau/plenary scheduling, and the Foreign Exchange Code's floor vote.

The ONAT assembly's own outcome remains unreported, but this run's research surfaced and corrected a citation-hygiene issue: recent changelog entries (Runs 439, 440, 443, 444) had drifted into describing Bâtonnier Boubaker Bethabet's election as having occurred "May 1, 2026" — this is the previously-flagged Run 423 false-positive trap resurfacing (the May 1, 2026 event was an extraordinary general assembly's strike vote, not an election); independently re-verified via Kapitalis, webdo, Jeune Afrique, and African Manager that Bethabet was in fact elected bâtonnier on September 13, 2025 for a 2025–2028 term, matching the standalone Judicial Statute entry's existing (and correct) Run 46 sourcing — no live law-card content was wrong, only this changelog's own recent narration; flagged here so future runs stop repeating the drifted date. A lateral sweep reconfirmed the CPG/GCT loan-guarantee bills (n° 49–51/2026) remained at Finance and Budget Commission examination as of July 21, 2026 (La Presse de Tunisie), still committee-stage, not a new development. No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication was identified beyond what is already tracked. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required substantive revision (310 total remains accurate) — a pure confirmation-and-hygiene run. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 310 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, and all three version markers synchronized at v4.447/Run 447. No PushNotification sent: a third corroborating source for an already-resolved item and a changelog-only date correction do not meet the bar for the user's attention. A general sweep for JORT publications and new legislation dated July 19–21, 2026 found none beyond what is already tracked; a July 6, 2026 Business News fact-check debunking a rumored July 1, 2026 Traffic Code entry-into-force (the 1999 code, Law 99-71, remains in force) was noted as a non-development. No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication was identified.   • (v4.450 update, Run 450): This run's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-jbmpz1) again held only a stale, disconnected v2.20-era lineage (183 entries) sharing no history with origin/main's independent progress to v4.449 (310 entries, 449 merged runs) — the same recurring failure mode documented throughout this changelog. This time a full research pass had already been drafted against the stale base and opened as PR #486 (sole finding: the Development Plan's July 20 presidential promulgation); the merge attempt correctly failed with a "405 merge conflicts" error, the finding was confirmed already present at greater precision in the real artifact (Development Plan 2026–2030, documented since Run 446 with four corroborating sources by Run 448 versus this draft's two), PR #486 was closed unmerged with an explanatory comment, and the branch was hard-reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B plus a force-with-lease push before restarting. A fresh WebSearch pass then re-checked four items from the standing watchlist: the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution — still unreported by any outlet checked, now 15 days past its July 6, 2026 hearing; the ONAT assembly outcome — still no July 2026 general-assembly result found, only the already-documented May 2026 protest/strike cycle; the private higher-education bill — still awaiting ARP Bureau plenary scheduling, no date set, consistent with the existing Run 437 entry; and the Development Plan's JORT citation — a WebSearch AI summary surfaced a specific-looking claim ("adopted by law n°2026-280 of May 7, 2026") that was investigated and rejected as non-credible: the cited date precedes the ARP's own July 10, 2026 floor vote on the bill by over two months, and "2026-280" is an implausibly high sequential number for a law dated that early in the year given this artifact's own documented numbering patterns (contemporaneous 2026 laws run in the single/low-double digits) — likely a search-summarizer hallucination or cross-jurisdiction mix-up (one of the same search's results was an unrelated Côte d'Ivoire Council of Ministers communiqué), consistent with this changelog's previously-documented "Moroccan-content trap" pattern (Run 135/429); not added. No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication was identified this pass. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required substantive revision (310 total remains accurate) — a pure branch-hygiene-and-confirmation run. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 310 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, 310 <h3> tags, 1,450/1,450 balanced <div> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, and all three version markers synchronized at v4.450/Run 450. A full draft had already been opened as PR #487 (two findings: the Development Plan's presidential promulgation, and a new pending-reform card for Minister Wajdi Hedhili's collective-lands-in-southern-Tunisia decree-law); the merge attempt correctly failed with a "405 merge conflicts" error, both findings were confirmed already present at greater precision in the real artifact (Development Plan 2026–2030, documented since Run 446 with four corroborating sources by Run 448; the collective-lands reform, added as the standalone State Domains Code & Collective Land Decree-Law entry since Run 306), PR #487 was closed unmerged with an explanatory comment, and the branch was hard-reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B before restarting. One genuine, minor finding: a fifth independent outlet, L'Économiste Maghrébin (July 21, 2026), was located independently referencing the Development Plan's promulgation in the same already-promulgated terms as the four sources found by Run 446–448 — added as an in-place enrichment to the Development Plan entry; the exact JORT issue/decree-number citation remains the entry's sole open item despite renewed site-restricted searches of jort.tn and 9anoun.tn. The remaining eight watchlist items were reconfirmed unchanged, each via a fresh search rather than an assumption: the ONAT general-assembly outcome remains unreported by any outlet, now 10+ days past the July 11, 2026 assembly; the Mseddi/Ennahdha bill still has no confirmed committee referral since its July 9, 2026 filing; the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome remains unreported since its July 6, 2026 hearing (Mnemty president Saadia Mosbah's separate, unrelated money-laundering conviction was independently reconfirmed upheld on appeal June 23, 2026 — not to be conflated with the dissolution case); the private higher-education bill remains at the same "awaiting ARP Bureau plenary scheduling" stage as Run 437, the only candidate "movement" article found being a stale June 18, 2026 piece; the telework/platform-economy bills still show no formal ARP filing (search yield was thin — flagged for a distinct-phrasing retry next run); the diaspora-council bill's July 14, 2026 committee adoption was reconfirmed, with a caution added to this note for future runs not to overread a webdo.tn headline ("L'ARP approuve...") as plenary passage — the article's own body, quoting rapporteur Taïeb Talbi, confirms only that the committee voted to transmit the text to the ARP Bureau, a step short of scheduling; and the Foreign Exchange Code committee review remains at the same ~90%-complete, no-floor-vote stage per committee chair Maher Ketari's June 17, 2026 statement. A lateral sweep for any wholly new JORT publication or enacted law dated July 19–21, 2026 found nothing beyond the already-tracked Development Plan item; direct browsing of jort.tn (rather than relying on search-engine indexing, which appears to lag recent issues) is recommended for a future run. No new standalone entries were added (310 total remains accurate); one existing entry (Development Plan) enriched with a fifth corroborating source. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 310 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, 310 <h3> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, and balanced <div> tags; all three version markers synchronized at v4.451/Run 451. and the single-use-plastics bill remains under ARP review at the deposit stage with no floor vote. A separate check of a July 2026 Council of Ministers session (Fidaa Foundation decree-law amendment, Aghlabides Medical City decree, AfDB agroforestry loan bill, IFAD financing-agreement bill) found only routine administrative/decree-level items outside the artifact's legislative-code scope, consistent with the project's established scoping. A dedicated research agent re-checked the full ten-item standing watchlist plus ran a general July 20–22, 2026 news sweep and a five-topic gap-scan (cooperatives law, whistleblower protection, urban transport/VTC regulation, gambling/betting reform, tax adviser organic law). All ten watchlist items were reconfirmed unchanged in substance: LTDH appeal (still adjourned to July 29, 2026), Ennahdha/Mseddi bill (still no committee referral), Foreign Exchange Code (still Finance Committee stage, BCT non-responsive), Personal Data Protection/AI bill (still pending; the regulations.ai "July 11 full enforcement" claim again did not resurface and remains excluded as uncorroborated), Audiovisual Communication bill (no movement beyond the May 20 jurisdictional dispute), Investment Code reform (continuing article-by-article voting, with a further session confirmed July 13, 2026), ONAT (no new general assembly found; the agent separately flagged that the artifact's "ONAT" watchlist note may be conflating the lawyers' bar association with the OECT accountants' order — a pre-existing distinction already correctly maintained in the standalone Lawyers Statute entry since Run 170, so no change was needed), Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution proceedings (still unresolved since the July 6 hearing), and the single-use-plastics bill (still under committee review, no floor vote). The 2026–2030 Development Plan's JORT citation remains untraceable despite a further attempted direct fetch of iort.gov.tn (HTTP 503), though a July 21, 2026 Carthage Palace meeting report (La Presse, African Manager) further corroborates the promulgation already on file. One genuine, previously-unresolved enrichment was closed: the Telecommunications Code Art. 86 entry's Belloumi/Boughdiri prosecution (Run 393) had been left with its July 21, 2026 opposition-hearing outcome "not yet known" — that hearing has now concluded, with the Tunis Court of Appeal's correctional chamber accepting the opposition, annulling the in-absentia judgment against both journalists, and referring the case back for full re-examination, with a new hearing set for December 2, 2026; added as a Run 463 update, corroborated by La Presse, Directinfo/WebManagerCenter, and Kapitalis. Two other leads were checked and correctly not added: an ISIE-announced July 20, 2026 partial-legislative-election result for El Kabaria (Tunis) is an administrative/electoral outcome rather than a legislative development, outside this artifact's scope; and the ARP External Relations Committee's July 14, 2026 adoption of amendments to the Conseil national des Tunisiens résidant à l'étranger reform bill was found already captured at equal precision in the existing entry's changelog prose (Run 311 update). No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required further revision beyond the one enrichment (310 total remains accurate). A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 310 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, balanced <div> and <section> tags, and all three version markers synchronized at v4.463/Run 463. A dedicated research sub-agent re-checked the full eleven-item standing watchlist via fresh English/French/Arabic WebSearch, plus a lateral gap-scan for anything newly enacted, filed, or ruled between July 20–22, 2026. All eleven items reconfirmed unchanged from the Run 467 baseline: Development Plan 2026–2030 (both chambers adopted, press still calling it "définitivement adopté," but no JORT issue/decree-number citation found in any source — the most recently indexed JORT issue via 9anoun.tn remains n° 2026-035 of April 3, 2026, well before the Plan's adoption, suggesting indexing lag rather than confirming the promulgation gazette entry hasn't yet issued); LTDH appeal (independently reconfirmed via Kapitalis, Réalités, businessnews.com.tn, African Manager, and Tunisie Tribune as still adjourned to July 29, 2026, not yet due); ONAT general assembly (July 11, 2026 outcome still unreported by any outlet, including a direct Arabic-language search, now fifteen-plus consecutive runs unresolved); Ennahdha/Mseddi bill (a July 12, 2026 Arabic-language source confirms the bill still "awaits referral to the competent parliamentary committees" as of that date, with no referral news since); Foreign Exchange Code (still Finance Committee stage under Maher Ktari, no vote); Personal Data Protection/AI bill (still pending since the May 20 Rights & Liberties Committee session; a fresh African Manager source describes the text as "123 articles across 6 titles," which reads as support for the 123 figure but is the same May 20 session already on file, not a new primary clarification — the 123-vs-132 dispute is left unresolved per standing practice); Audiovisual Communication bill (jurisdictional dispute over committee assignment still unresolved since May 20); Investment Code reform (still article-by-article voting under Saber Jelassi, latest confirmed committee activity end-May/early-June); Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution (July 6, 2026 hearing outcome still unreported; a distinct June 23, 2026 Tunis Court of Appeal ruling upholding Mnemty president Saadia Mosbah's 8-year sentence in a separate money-laundering case was correctly not conflated with the dissolution proceeding); single-use-plastics bill (n° 33/2026, no committee action found after the June 25 hearing); and the telework/platform-economy bills (still "being finalized" per Minister Lahmar's July 20 remarks at the 8th Arab Conference on Pensions and Social Insurance, with UGTT pushing for consultation). The gap-scan's sole candidate — the three accelerated-procedure Finance Committee bills (n° 49–51/2026, EBRD/ITFC guarantee agreements for CPG and Groupe Chimique Tunisien) — is the same item already identified and correctly excluded as a routine sovereign-guarantee financing instrument at Runs 462, 464, and 467; no new JORT publication, enacted law, or plenary vote was found for July 20–22, 2026. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required revision (310 total remains accurate) — a pure branch-hygiene-and-confirmation run. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 310 unique law-card ids (310 <h3> tags; the sole id="renewables-law" duplicate hit from a naive grep was re-confirmed as the known false-positive of a <code>-quoted changelog reference, not a second card), balanced <div> (1450/1450) and <section> (30/30) tags, and all three version markers synchronized at v4.468/Run 468. No PushNotification sent: a twelfth consecutive unchanged-watchlist reconfirmation with the sole gap-scan lead already out of scope does not meet the bar for the user's attention. Standing priorities for the next run remain unchanged from Run 467.
  • (v4.469 update, Run 469): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-5y9uyd) again held only a stale, disconnected v2.22-era snapshot (186 entries, from PR #500 merged long ago) sharing no further history with origin/main's independent progress to v4.468 (310 entries, 468 merged runs) — the same recurring failure mode documented since Run 87. This time the discrepancy was caught only after a full research pass had already been drafted against the stale base and opened as PR #514 (candidate findings: Decree-Law No. 2022-14 on illegal speculation, a new telework/digital-platform-economy pending-reform entry, and an "ARP bill no. 42/2026" citation added to the Development Plan entry); the merge attempt correctly failed with a "405 merge conflicts" error, PR #514 was closed unmerged with an explanatory comment, and the branch was hard-reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B before restarting, per established practice. Each candidate finding was then cross-checked against the real current artifact rather than blindly re-added: Decree-Law No. 2022-14 was confirmed already present (added at Run 2, refined at Run 157); the telework/digital-platform-economy bills were confirmed already documented in the Digital Platform Workers entry (added Run 99, updated through Run 466); and the "n° 42/2026" bill-number citation for the Development Plan was confirmed to be exactly the fabrication already identified and discarded at Run 460 (a conflation with the bill's original June 17, 2026 ARP-deposit number, not a promulgation citation) — correctly not re-added. A fresh research pass then re-checked the eleven-item standing watchlist via WebSearch: the Development Plan's JORT issue/decree-number citation remains untraceable (both chambers' adoption reconfirmed, still only "récemment promulguée" framing in every outlet checked); the ONAT general assembly outcome remains unreported (now sixteen-plus consecutive runs unresolved); the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution case remains unresolved since its July 6, 2026 hearing; and the LTDH appeal remains correctly not yet due (July 29, 2026). One candidate lead from the gap-scan was investigated and correctly excluded: a July 21, 2026 Le Temps News report on "le CNOT tient son Assemblée générale annuelle" is the Comité National Olympique Tunisien's own unrelated annual assembly, not the long-unresolved ONAT (Ordre National des Avocats de Tunisie) general assembly — a name-confusion trap distinct from, but structurally similar to, the false-positive patterns flagged throughout this changelog. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required substantive revision (310 total remains accurate) — a pure branch-hygiene-and-confirmation run. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 310 unique law-card ids (310 <h3> tags), balanced <div> (1450/1450) and <section> (30/30) tags, and all three version markers synchronized at v4.469/Run 469. No PushNotification sent: this run's only genuinely new information was confirming that its own draft's candidate findings were already covered, which is a housekeeping outcome, not a development requiring the user's attention. Standing priorities for the next run remain unchanged: the Development Plan's JORT issue/decree-number citation, the LTDH appeal ruling (due July 29, 2026), the ONAT assembly outcome, the Mseddi bill's committee referral, the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome, the telework/platform-economy bills' formal ARP filing, and the Foreign Exchange Code's floor vote.

The open-ended sweep's sole tangential lead — a July 9, 2026 petition from 52 ARP deputies pressing the government to publish implementing decrees for the long-term-unemployed-graduates recruitment law (Law No. 2025-18) — was cross-checked and found already fully documented in the existing standalone entry's Run 201 update; no addition was needed. No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication was identified. The cross-check found: the Djemali/Krimi case already present, with materially more precision (added at Run 106) — not re-added; the Chadha Hadj Mbarek fact already captured in outline within the Instalingo case (Run 149's "reducing one female defendant's sentence and ordering her release") but left unnamed — now identified by name and cross-checked against five independent sources (Kapitalis, L'Économiste Maghrébin, Le Courrier de l'Atlas, Tunisie Tribune, Espace Manager), added as an enrichment rather than a new card; and the Saloua Ghrissa case (executive director of the Association for the Promotion of the Right to Difference, held over fifteen months in pretrial detention on foreign-funding allegations before her March 19, 2026 provisional release) confirmed genuinely absent from all 476 prior runs despite the artifact's otherwise thorough coverage of the same 2024–2026 civil-society prosecution wave (Djemali/Krimi, Abdallah Saïd, Tunisie Terre d'Asile) — added as a new standalone entry, cross-checked against Amnesty International, Human Rights Watch, Business News, Nawaat, and Kapitalis, and cross-referenced from the Administrative Suspensions entry. One new standalone entry added (311 total, up from 310); two existing entries (Instalingo/conspiracy-cases, civil-society-suspensions) enriched with cross-references. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 311 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, 311 <h3> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, 1454/1454 balanced <div> tags, and all three version markers synchronized at v4.477/Run 477. Standing priorities for the next run remain unchanged: the Development Plan's JORT issue/decree-number citation, the LTDH appeal ruling (due July 29, 2026), the ONAT assembly outcome, the Mseddi bill's committee referral, the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome, the telework/platform-economy bills' formal ARP filing, and the Foreign Exchange Code's floor vote.

A dedicated research sub-agent re-checked the full seven-item standing watchlist via fresh WebSearch/WebFetch. All seven items reconfirmed unchanged from the Run 477 baseline: Development Plan 2026–2030 (a direct fetch of L'Economiste Maghrébin's July 19, 2026 "définitivement adopté" piece re-confirmed it contains no JORT issue number, law number, or decree citation — a 22nd consecutive run without one); LTDH appeal (independently reconfirmed via African Manager and WebManagerCenter's July 16, 2026 coverage as still adjourned to July 29, 2026 pending the state-litigation attorney general's response, not yet due); ONAT general assembly (July 11, 2026 outcome still unreported by any outlet; one African Manager headline referencing an "assemblée générale élective" was checked and found to be recirculated coverage of the September 2025 bâtonnier election, not the July 11, 2026 ordinary assembly — correctly not treated as a resolution, now twenty-three consecutive runs unresolved); Ennahdha/Mseddi bill (still no committee referral since its July 9, 2026 filing); Foreign Exchange Code (still Finance/Planning Committee stage; the CONECT 41-amendment hearing of ~June 1–5, 2026 remains the most recent movement, no BCT engagement or floor vote reported); Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution (no ruling found; the most recent confirmed hearing remains the May 11 → June 1, 2026 postponement, with no independent confirmation of a subsequent July 6 hearing surfacing this pass); and the telework/platform-economy bills (Minister Issam Lahmar again called both "nearly finalized" at the July 20–22, 2026 8th Arab Conference on Pensions and Social Insurance per Managers.tn and Business News, still not formally filed at the ARP). An open-ended sweep for anything newly enacted, filed, or ruled since July 21–22, 2026 found no JORT publication, ARP floor vote, or major court ruling; the only ARP activity identified was a deputies' petition for a plenary session on summer power cuts (not a vote) and the same out-of-scope CPG/GCT phosphate-financing committee hearing (bills 49–51/2026) already excluded at Runs 452–453. Two candidate leads the sub-agent flagged as "possibly uncovered" were independently cross-checked against the live artifact via targeted grep and found already fully documented: the single-use-plastics bill (n° 33/2026 — standalone entry, synced with its bill number and committee status at Run 475) and the National Council for Tunisians Residing Abroad reform bill (n° 2024/064 — standalone entry, updated through Run 335) — neither required any change. No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication was identified. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required revision (311 total remains accurate) — a pure branch-hygiene-and-confirmation run. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 311 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, 311 <h3> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, 1454/1454 balanced <div> tags, and all three version markers synchronized at v4.478/Run 478. No PushNotification sent: a seventeenth consecutive unchanged-watchlist reconfirmation, with both open-ended-sweep leads already on file at equal or greater precision, does not meet the bar for the user's attention.   • (v4.479 update, Run 479): Branch checked out clean at current origin/main (v4.478, 311 entries, 478 merged runs) — no stale-lineage issue this run. A dedicated research sub-agent re-checked the full seven-item standing watchlist via fresh, distinctly-phrased WebSearch/WebFetch queries, plus an open-ended sweep for anything newly enacted July 21–23, 2026. All seven items remain open, none resolved: the Development Plan 2026–2030 saw its one substantive development already on file since Run 446 re-confirmed — ARP plenary approval (July 10, 64–24–15) and CNRD final approval (July 17, 56–6–5) are both complete, and President Saïed's July 20–21 public remarks (reviewing implementation with the PM, Economy Minister, and BCT governor, and calling to convert debt into investment) treat the plan as adopted — but a direct fetch of L'Economiste Maghrébin's "définitivement adopté" coverage again contained no JORT issue number or law/decree citation, a 23rd consecutive run without one. The LTDH appeal remains adjourned to July 29, 2026 (not yet due). The ONAT general assembly outcome remains unreported by any outlet under further distinctly-phrased queries, now twenty-four consecutive runs unresolved. The Mseddi/Ennahdha bill still shows no confirmed committee referral — one retrospective summary noted only that the proposal "must still gather sufficient signatures to be examined in commission," background context rather than a referral report. The Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution case remains unreported beyond the already-documented May 11 → June 1 → July 6, 2026 postponement chain; no outcome or further postponement date was found for the period after July 6. The telework/platform-economy bills remain "nearly finalized" per Minister Issam Lahmar's July 20–22, 2026 remarks at the 8th Arab Conference on Pensions and Social Insurance, still with no formal ARP bureau deposit reported. The Foreign Exchange Code remains at Finance/Planning Committee stage, with no confirmation that the article-by-article committee vote (targeted for completion by late June per committee chair Maher Ktari) concluded or that a plenary referral occurred. The open-ended sweep found no newly enacted law, decree-law, or JORT publication dated July 21–23, 2026; the only ARP activity identified was the same out-of-scope CPG/GCT phosphate-financing hearing (bills 49–51/2026, July 21 session) already excluded at Runs 452–453, plus a committee-level (not yet plenary) adoption of amendments restructuring the Conseil National des Tunisiens à l'Étranger under the Presidency of Government, which duplicates the existing diaspora-council entry's tracked status. No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication was identified. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required revision (311 total remains accurate) — a pure confirmation run. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 311 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, 311 <h3> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, 1454/1454 balanced <div> tags, and all version markers synchronized at v4.479/Run 479.   • (v4.481 update, Run 481): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-yeglol) initially held only a stale, disconnected v2.24-era snapshot (from PR #523 merged long ago, 188 entries) sharing no further history with origin/main's independent progress to v4.480 (311 entries, 480 merged runs) — the same recurring failure mode documented since Run 87; a first drafting pass was begun against that stale base before git fetch origin main and git log surfaced the discrepancy, and the branch was hard-reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B before any commit was made, avoiding the wasted-draft/PR-conflict failure mode some prior runs hit. A dedicated research sub-agent plus direct WebSearch/WebFetch verification then re-checked the full seven-item standing watchlist: the Development Plan 2026–2030 JORT citation remains unfound (a 25th consecutive run without one); the LTDH appeal remains adjourned to July 29, 2026, not yet due; the ONAT general assembly outcome remains unreported by any outlet, now twenty-six consecutive runs unresolved; the Mseddi/Ennahdha bill still shows no confirmed committee referral since its July 9, 2026 filing; the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution case remains unreported beyond the July 6, 2026 hearing; the telework/platform-economy bills remain "quasiment finalisés" per Minister Issam Lahmar's July 20–22, 2026 remarks, still not formally filed at the ARP; and the Foreign Exchange Code remains at Finance/Planning Committee stage with no floor vote. Three genuine gaps were found and closed, all cross-checked against at least two independently dated sources via direct WebFetch of primary articles (not just search snippets). (1) A new standalone Posidonia (Seagrass) Protection Bill entry (Proposition de loi n° 118/2025) — heard by the Industry/Commerce/Environment Committee on the same June 25, 2026 date as the already-tracked plastic-bags bill, the legislative component of the FFEM/WWF France "PENELOPE" blue-carbon project, entirely absent from 480 prior runs despite the shared hearing date. (2) A new standalone Draft Finance Law 2027 (PLF 2027) budget-dialogue entry: UGTT's early-July 2026 formal tax/budget proposals (raising the tax-exempt threshold from TND 5,000 to 7,000, IRPP bracket revision) and the Finance Ministry registry office's refusal to formally receive UGTT's document, which UGTT Secretary-General Slaheddine Selmi called a setback for social dialogue. (3) A substantial enrichment to the existing Decree-Law No. 2022-54 entry, whose amendment-attempt history had only been summarized as "announced in December 2025" — now documents the attempt's full timeline: filed February 20, 2024, resubmitted by 60 deputies January 2025, General Legislation Committee hearings opening July 2, 2025 under then-chair Yassine Kourrari, the specific articles targeted (5, 6, 9, 10, 11, 12, plus repeal of Art. 24 itself), continuation under new chair Faouzi Daâs from January 2026, and a March 27, 2026 hearing taking National Bar Association testimony, with the outcome still "uncertain" as of the most recent reporting found and no further committee activity through this pass. One dating error was caught before publication: an initial search snippet had misdated the July 2, 2025 committee hearing as "July 2, 2026" (omitting the year); direct verification — the source specifies "Wednesday, July 2," which is 2025, not the Thursday that July 2, 2026 falls on — corrected this before it entered the artifact. No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication was identified this pass. Two new standalone entries added (313 total, up from 311); one existing entry substantially enriched. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 313 unique law-card ids (the sole id="renewables-law" grep hit re-confirmed as the known false-positive of a <code>-quoted changelog reference, not a second card), 313 <h3> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, 1462/1462 balanced <div> tags, and all three version markers synchronized at v4.481/Run 481. Standing priorities for the next run remain unchanged: the Development Plan's JORT issue/decree-number citation, the LTDH appeal ruling (due July 29, 2026), the ONAT assembly outcome, the Mseddi bill's committee referral, the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome, the telework/platform-economy bills' formal ARP filing, and the Foreign Exchange Code's floor vote.

The one substantive lead from the discarded draft — a candidate JORT citation for the Development Plan 2026–2030 — was re-verified rather than discarded outright: four independently-phrased WebSearch queries consistently returned Law No. 2026-16 of July 20, 2026 (JORT July 21, 2026), but all four traced to the same single outlet (Business News, July 22, 2026), four direct WebFetch attempts on that article each returned HTTP 503, and neither a second independent outlet nor a direct jort.tn/9anoun.tn hit could be found to corroborate the specific number — so, consistent with this artifact's two-independent-source rule and its past experience rejecting a fabricated aggregator citation on the Data Protection entry, it was added to the entry as an explicitly flagged, unconfirmed lead for next-run follow-up rather than as a settled citation (a 30th consecutive run without a confirmed citation, though the first with a specific number to chase down). A fresh WebSearch sweep then independently re-checked the other six standing-watchlist items with no change found: LTDH (still adjourned to July 29, 2026, not yet due), ONAT (still unreported, now 31 consecutive runs unresolved), Al Khatt/Mnemty (still unreported beyond a hearing date — this run's search surfaced only the already-superseded May 11/June 1, 2026 postponement, no ruling), Mseddi/Ennahdha bill (still no committee referral beyond its July 9, 2026 filing), telework/platform-economy bills (still "nearly finalized" per Minister Lahmar, not yet formally filed), and the Foreign Exchange Code (still ~90%-complete committee hearings, BCT non-response, no floor vote). No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication was otherwise identified. No new standalone entries were added (313 total remains accurate) — this run enriched one existing entry with a flagged lead rather than adding or moving a card. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 313 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, 313 <h3> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, 1462/1462 balanced <div> tags, and all three version markers synchronized at v4.486/Run 486. No PushNotification sent: an unconfirmed single-source lead and a further unchanged-watchlist reconfirmation does not meet the bar for the user's attention until the citation is independently corroborated. Standing priorities for the next run: attempt the Business News direct fetch again (or find a mirror/second outlet) to confirm or reject Law No. 2026-16, plus the unchanged six-item watchlist above.
  • (v4.487 update, Run 487): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-bjkhjv) again initially held only a stale, disconnected v2.24-era snapshot (from PR #523, merged long ago, 188 entries) — a full v2.25 draft (moving the Development Plan entry to Administrative Law on an unconfirmed citation, correcting the Constitutional Court entry's last-hearing date, and adding a Zeghidi/Bsaies press entry and an Al Khatt/Mnemty note) had already been committed and pushed against that stale base, and a PR (#542) opened; the merge attempt correctly returned a "dirty" mergeable_state, which caught origin/main's independent progress to v4.486 (313 entries, 486 merged runs) — the same recurring failure mode documented since Run 87. All four candidate findings from the discarded draft were cross-checked against the real current artifact via targeted grep before anything was reused, and all four were found already present, in every case at materially greater precision: the Development Plan/Law No. 2026-16 lead (already tracked, explicitly flagged as unconfirmed, at Run 486); the Constitutional Court bill's March 27, 2026 ONAT hearing (already documented at Run 187, with the ONAT delegation's specific all-magistrate-composition objection and the bâtonnier's Conseil Supérieur de la Magistrature remark — detail the discarded draft's independent research had not recovered); the Zeghidi/Bsaies money-laundering prosecution (already documented in the Decree-Law 2022-54 entry since Run 39/40); and the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution wave (already tracked in the "Administrative Suspensions & Judicial Dissolution Proceedings" entry across many runs, most recently reconfirmed at Run 485). None were re-added. A fresh WebSearch sweep then independently re-checked the full seven-item standing watchlist, including a further attempt to corroborate Law No. 2026-16: the Business News article again returned HTTP 503 on direct fetch, and a dedicated check of a second outlet's coverage of the same July 20 Carthage Palace/JORT news (Entreprises Magazine) confirmed the ARP/CNRD vote tallies but, on direct fetch, contained no law number or JORT citation of its own — so the specific "2026-16" number remains single-sourced and is left exactly as Run 486 flagged it (unconfirmed, a 31st consecutive run without a corroborated citation). The other six items showed no movement: LTDH (still adjourned to July 29, 2026, not yet due), ONAT (still unreported, now 32 consecutive runs unresolved), Al Khatt/Mnemty (still unreported beyond the July 6, 2026 hearing date, no verdict located), Mseddi/Ennahdha bill (still no committee referral beyond its July 9, 2026 filing, despite continued op-ed commentary through July 13), telework/platform-economy bills (per Minister Issam Lahmar's remarks at the July 20–22, 2026 Arab Conference on Pensions and Social Insurance, still "nearing completion," not yet formally filed at the ARP), and the Foreign Exchange Code (still ~90%-complete committee hearings, BCT non-response, no floor vote; the July 21, 2026 plenary session found by this run's search concerned only the unrelated STEG/Clean Technology Fund World Bank guarantee bills, already excluded from this artifact's scope as routine sovereign financing). A broader sweep for any newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication dated July 22–23, 2026 found nothing beyond what Run 486 already captured. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required revision (313 total remains accurate) — a branch-hygiene-and-confirmation run. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 313 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, 313 <h3> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, and all three version markers synchronized at v4.487/Run 487. Two candidate findings from the sub-agent's sweep were cross-checked against the real current artifact via targeted grep and found already fully captured, at equal or greater precision, rather than genuine gaps: the Law No. 2026-16 (Development Plan 2026–2030) JORT-publication citation the sub-agent surfaced independently duplicates the confirmation already made in-place at Run 488 (same two outlets, same law number and dates); and a single-sourced businessnews.com.tn lead on a Tunis Court of First Instance hearing postponing a prosecutorial challenge to ONAT's May 1, 2026 extraordinary general assembly to October 28, 2026 duplicates the Lawyers Statute entry's own Run 249 addition (same October 28, 2026 date, same 580-lawyer acte de constitution detail), which is in fact more precise than the sub-agent's independent research. No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication dated July 22–23, 2026 was identified beyond what Run 488 already captured. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required revision (313 total remains accurate) — a pure confirmation run. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 313 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, 313 <h3> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, 1462/1462 balanced <div> tags, and all three version markers synchronized at v4.489/Run 489. No PushNotification sent: a further unchanged-watchlist reconfirmation, with both sub-agent leads already on file at equal or greater precision, does not meet the bar for the user's attention.   • (v4.490 update, Run 490): Branch checked out clean at current origin/main (v4.489, 313 entries, 489 merged runs) — no stale-lineage issue this run. All six items remain unchanged: the LTDH appeal remains adjourned to July 29, 2026 (not yet due), reconfirmed via African Manager and Tunisie Tribune (both July 16); the ONAT general-assembly-legality challenge, heard June 10, 2026, was itself adjourned to October 28, 2026 (already documented in the Bar Law entry since Run 249) — no near-term ruling expected before then, now 35 consecutive runs without a separate outcome; the Mseddi/Ennahdha bill still shows no confirmed committee referral since its July 9, 2026 filing; the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution case remains unreported beyond the July 6, 2026 hearing (independently re-checked against Inkyfada, RSF, ICIJ, Businessnews, and Directinfo, none reporting a verdict); the telework/platform-economy bills remain "nearly finalized" per Minister Issam Lahmar's July 20–22, 2026 remarks, corroborated by four independent outlets (Managers.tn, La Presse, Businessnews, Directinfo), still not formally filed at the ARP; and the Foreign Exchange Code committee remains awaiting BCT input as of a June 23, 2026 Businessnews report citing the committee chair directly, no vote or plenary referral since. Two candidate findings from the sub-agent's broad sweep were cross-checked against the real current artifact via targeted grep and confirmed already fully captured rather than genuine gaps: the Law No. 2026-16 (Development Plan 2026–2030) JORT-publication citation duplicates the confirmation already made in-place at Run 488, reconfirmed again at Run 489; and the Abdennaceur Aouini appeal-conviction case (July 15, 2026 ruling) duplicates the artifact's own standalone entry, on file since Run 367. No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication dated July 22–23, 2026 was identified beyond what Runs 488–489 already captured. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required revision (313 total remains accurate) — a pure confirmation run. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 313 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, 313 <h3> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, and all three version markers synchronized at v4.490/Run 490. No PushNotification sent: a further unchanged-watchlist reconfirmation, with both sub-agent leads already on file at equal or greater precision, does not meet the bar for the user's attention. A fresh WebSearch sweep re-checked the full six-item standing watchlist with no change found: LTDH (adjourned to July 29, 2026, not yet due); ONAT (adjourned to October 28, 2026, per the Bar Law entry); Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution (unreported beyond the July 6, 2026 hearing); Mseddi/Ennahdha bill (no confirmed committee referral since its July 9, 2026 filing); telework/platform-economy bills (still "nearly finalized" per Minister Issam Lahmar's July 20–22, 2026 remarks, not yet filed at the ARP); and the Foreign Exchange Code (committee still awaiting BCT input, no floor vote). This run's genuine contribution was outside the watchlist: a July 19, 2026 CRLDHT statement (published by Kapitalis, July 21) on the July 13, 2026 appellate sentence increase for former prosecutor Béchir Akremi (23→29 years, already documented at the Chokri Belaïd Assassination Trial entry) was traced to a broader civil-society framing of the ruling as part of a systemic pattern of judicial reprisals — added as an enrichment to the Judicial Statute entry alongside a previously undocumented case it cites: magistrate Hamadi Rahmani, prosecuted since December 2024 on six social-media-defamation complaints filed by former Court of Cassation president Taïeb Rached, released on provisional liberty, with an unresolved judicial-immunity dispute (corroborated via tunisienumerique.com and African Manager). No new standalone entries were added (313 total remains accurate, since the finding extends an existing card). A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 313 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, 313 <h3> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, 1462/1462 balanced <div> tags, and all three version markers synchronized at v4.492/Run 492. No PushNotification sent: an enrichment to an already-documented persecution pattern, alongside a further unchanged-watchlist reconfirmation, does not meet the bar for the user's attention. Standing priorities for the next run remain unchanged: the LTDH appeal ruling (due July 29, 2026), the ONAT general-assembly-legality ruling (due October 28, 2026), the Mseddi bill's committee referral, the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution outcome, the telework/platform-economy bills' formal ARP filing, and the Foreign Exchange Code's floor vote.
